I've read too much rubbish on the internet & my family & friends are starting to think I've gone mad (so he says)
I'm going home in couple of weeks & be there for a month then coming back up. think it might be a long month. He still won't consider moving - says he would never be happy anywhere else & he'd only make my life & his miserable which is probably right!
I'm thinking of asking Josh (paleo osteo) for a certificate of saneness & stating that I am doing the only thing possible to get healthy & maintain it.

### caroline — 2015-11-19T10:47:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-forums-been-lurking-in-background-too-long.12652/post-182316>

A lot of manipulation going on .....so sorry Jenny

When we moved to Australia from Canada - a lot of folks asked us how we could do that etc. etc. - such a big move and weren't we scared and on and on. A close friend who is a therapist wanted to have a long talk to me and did I understand what I was doing and how huge a move it was.

My answer always was ..... It is just geography - wherever my DH is ..... is home. That is all that really mattered to me.

All the new shared experiences are magic.

Please remember to care care of you first and foremost and he has to do the same. To say he wouldn't be happy anywhere else is pretty extreme ....and amazingly boring!
xo
I will pm my phone number - call me when you get back this way. I would imagine it will be so stressful for you.

### Jenny S — 2015-11-20T08:51:57+0200

Thanks Endless - sounds like you've been in similar situation - man didn't change?
Interesting development from phone call husband few minutes ago, wants to buy a small place still in same town but thinks we might have to divorce as he can't cope with a part time wife. I will have to sell our home & deal with all the crap 'cos he can't. Not really enough money to buy 2 smaller places but as this has been my choice so far, so looks like it the decision might be easy as he's making it for me! Spose it's healthy living in a tent anyway!
Don't feel that happy right this minute but guess if that's what has to be I will cope as my decision in moving was choosing health over husband, guess that happiness is up to me ultimately ..... ### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2015-11-20T09:27:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-forums-been-lurking-in-background-too-long.12652/post-182410>

when one tells you they care/love you etc, you must ask yourself if it is selfish care/love or universal care/love.

he says he cant stand it without you, but will not change a thing in his own life to make sure he is there with you...

i see this as selfish love.

### Lahelada — 2015-11-20T13:59:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-forums-been-lurking-in-background-too-long.12652/post-182414>

### fitness@home — 2015-11-20T15:47:10+0200

### yarralea — 2016-03-04T01:21:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-forums-been-lurking-in-background-too-long.12652/post-190155>

> [Jenny S said:](/goto/post?id=153397)
>
> Hi all,
> I've been going to do this for ages, but long time coming.
> I bought Epi-Paleo RX about 6 mths ago - can't remember how I got to it, probably googling health issues.
> Had spiralling health issues for years, - like most people I guess. Lots of little issues didn't think much of at time, such as tiredness, tinnitus, bloating, constipation, dizziness, brain fog - put it all down to stress - even thought I didn't feel stressed. About 10 yrs ago started slowly losing weight & getting tireder & dizzy all time. Still nothing wrong after having gastroscopy & colonoscopy. Told no such thing as gluten intolerance, so kept eating gluten. I'm quite tall & about 2 yrs ago was only 44kgs. Luckily found GAPS diet, as GP just wanted to give me lots of drugs & put me in hospital which I refused - knew there must be more natural way.
> Have been on GAPS for 2 years & a lot better but still not as good as would have liked. Since following Jack's info have improved quite a bit. Find it all a bit much for the brain but after re-reading blogs over & over sometimes if lucky I have a light bulb moment!
> Think biggest improvements have probably come from eating fish by the ton, cutting out blue light at night & less EMF - one of the hardest things I've found to do.
> Finding it difficult being in Australia to sort some information such as water, fish & sun.
> Anyone out there from or near Bendigo? Would love to meet up with fellow Kruse followers as my husband, family & friends all think I'm nuts - don't really care about that, but nice to have someone else to meet & discuss things with!

Hiya Jenny!

I'm in Adelaide, well Burra really, so if you are ever this way, feel free to pop in and say hello.

### caroline — 2016-03-04T04:59:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-forums-been-lurking-in-background-too-long.12652/post-190162>

### Jenny S — 2016-03-16T04:03:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-forums-been-lurking-in-background-too-long.12652/post-190948>

Time for an update. Well I've really flown the coop. Have bought a place on Bribie Island, Queensland & moved about 10 days ago.
Finding it a bit tough, don't know anyone, family & friends & husband all back in Victoria. Husband thinks I've really let him down, not coping but won't move. I've decided it's the opposite. He's let me down, not supporting me in quest for health - doesn't even believe I'll be any better up here. I've given up trying to convince him & think it probably for best. Much easier to maintain healthy lifestyle without him undermining it with bright lights, tv, smart meters at our head etc, etc. ( & he's the one with cancer)
It will be hard for a while but know I will be better off in long run. Can't wait to be really healthy. Already noticed less tired (chronic fatigue) even tho not sleeping very well. Obviously stress with moving & separating don't help.
Unfortunately finances aren't same anymore & am going to have to look for a job, but at least it will be nice to be well enough to do so soon I hope.
Not sure what I shall look for - have banking background but last thing I want to do is go back to an office sitting in front of computer & under fake lights all day.
Thanks for your good wishes Audrey & Yarralea. I must ring Haley soon as she is probably only 1 hour drive away, tho won't go that far yet - too tired.
Interesting have noticed I can eat as much chocolate as I like even at night & doesn't affect me at all & nightshades don't worry me anymore. Obviously both good signs.

### Jenny S — 2016-07-15T10:03:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-forums-been-lurking-in-background-too-long.12652/post-198386>

Update - my B/C is now 19.8, down from 30.1 from 2 yrs ago, thought it might be be better than that, but suppose I should be grateful that it's going the right way. I can now turn of circuit breakers at night, but unfortunately have wi -fi 24/7 from neighbours which I can't do anything about.
Other than that it is all pretty easy now. Still battling with sleep issues but could be worse. So once again my heartfelt thanks to Jack & everyone else on forum as know I would be very unwell without all this information.
My other great news is I have met a guy who has never heard of Jack but knows about everything except blue light but more than willing to learn - he has just ordered blue blocking glasses, mobile phone covers etc. Same age as me & he has fallen under my spell - whoo hoo!!

### caroline — 2016-07-15T11:35:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-forums-been-lurking-in-background-too-long.12652/post-198389>

you already know how I feel about this great news!

### Jenny S — 2016-09-24T01:27:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-forums-been-lurking-in-background-too-long.12652/post-201831>

Quick update - after reading about Krista doing a fast mimicking diet I decided to try it. I just ate avocados, chocolate, nuts, some green powder & after 3 days lost 300g weight but 900 g fat. I had actually decided to stop doing it this morning & have - next time will try for full 5 days.

### Jenny S — 2016-09-24T23:59:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-forums-been-lurking-in-background-too-long.12652/post-201868>

Update - put half the fat back on but no weight after one day of normal diet.

# Thread: Seafood and Mercury risk:
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-and-mercury-risk.12663/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2015-01-22T03:33:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-and-mercury-risk.12663/post-153572>

As i have told all my members it is completely a fallacy of modern functional med docs and ancestral folks: Now we have some data points to support DHA.

Research abstract:[http://ajcn.nutrition.org/.../ajcn.114.100503.full.pdf...](http://ajcn.nutrition.org/content/early/2015/01/21/ajcn.114.100503.full.pdf+html?sid=e3cd9ff2-9092-40bb-a56e-4ad825d63fb7)

### Jack Kruse — 2015-01-22T03:34:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-and-mercury-risk.12663/post-153573>

Research from the same cohort:[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3208775/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3208775/). "For humans, fish is the primary source for some LCPUFA that are essential for neural development. The human body has a very limited ability to synthesize the n-3 LCPUFA docosahexaenoic acid (DHA) and eicosapentanoic acid (EPA), and fish is the primary human dietary source. ... The current findings are reassuring regarding the rewards and risks of fish consumption and indicate that exposure to MeHg during pregnancy from consumption of ocean fish is not adversely associated with neurocognitive and behavioral outcomes measured in late adolescence."

### Jack Kruse — 2015-01-22T03:35:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-and-mercury-risk.12663/post-153574>

[http://medicalxpress.com/news/2015-01-fatty-acids-fish-shield-brain.html](http://medicalxpress.com/news/2015-01-fatty-acids-fish-shield-brain.html)

### PaulG — 2015-01-22T13:26:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-and-mercury-risk.12663/post-153587>

This article dishes the n-3 brain evolution theory and makes some reasonable logical observations
[Has an aquatic diet been necessary for hominin brain evolution and functional development?](http://journals.cambridge.org/download.php?file=%2FBJN%2FBJN96_01%2FS0007114506001711a.pdf&code=9ae8aaa4ddce47a50e528452fc6608c7)

"A number of authors have argued that only an aquatic-based diet can provide the necessary quantity of DHA to support the human brain, and that a
switch to such a diet early in hominin evolution was critical to human brain evolution. This paper identifies the premises behind this hypothesis and
critiques them on the basis of clinical literature. Both tissue levels and certain functions of the developing infant brain are sensitive to extreme
variations in the supply of DHA in artificial feeding, and it can be shown that levels in human milk reflect maternal diet. However, both the
maternal and infant bodies have mechanisms to store and buffer the supply of DHA, so that functional deficits are generally resolved without
compensatory diets. There is no evidence that human diets based on terrestrial food chains with traditional nursing practices fail to provide adequate
levels of DHA or other n-3 fatty acids. Consequently, the hypothesis that DHA has been a limiting resource in human brain evolution must
be considered to be unsupported."

DHA, baby.

NSH

# Thread: RBM3, Cold and Neuroplasticity...Cold Shock Proteins
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rbm3-cold-and-neuroplasticity-cold-shock-proteins.12668/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Josh — 2015-01-22T22:42:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rbm3-cold-and-neuroplasticity-cold-shock-proteins.12668/post-153626>

> In the healthy adult brain synapses are continuously remodelled through a process of elimination and formation known as structural plasticity[1](http://www.nature.com/nature/journal/vaop/ncurrent/full/nature14142.html#ref1). Reduction in synapse number is a consistent early feature of neurodegenerative diseases[2](http://www.nature.com/nature/journal/vaop/ncurrent/full/nature14142.html#ref2), [3](http://www.nature.com/nature/journal/vaop/ncurrent/full/nature14142.html#ref3), suggesting deficient compensatory mechanisms. Although much is known about toxic processes leading to synaptic dysfunction and loss in these disorders[2](http://www.nature.com/nature/journal/vaop/ncurrent/full/nature14142.html#ref2), [3](http://www.nature.com/nature/journal/vaop/ncurrent/full/nature14142.html#ref3), how synaptic regeneration is affected is unknown. In hibernating mammals, cooling induces loss of synaptic contacts, which are reformed on rewarming, a form of structural plasticity[4](http://www.nature.com/nature/journal/vaop/ncurrent/full/nature14142.html#ref4), [5](http://www.nature.com/nature/journal/vaop/ncurrent/full/nature14142.html#ref5). We have found that similar changes occur in artificially cooled laboratory rodents. Cooling and hibernation also induce a number of cold-shock proteins in the brain, including the RNA binding protein, RBM3 (ref. [6](http://www.nature.com/nature/journal/vaop/ncurrent/full/nature14142.html#ref6)). The relationship of such proteins to structural plasticity is unknown. Here we show that synapse regeneration is impaired in mouse models of neurodegenerative disease, in association with the failure to induce RBM3. In both prion-infected and 5XFAD (Alzheimer-type) mice[7](http://www.nature.com/nature/journal/vaop/ncurrent/full/nature14142.html#ref7), the capacity to regenerate synapses after cooling declined in parallel with the loss of induction of RBM3. Enhanced expression of RBM3 in the hippocampus prevented this deficit and restored the capacity for synapse reassembly after cooling. RBM3 overexpression, achieved either by boosting endogenous levels through hypothermia before the loss of the RBM3 response or by lentiviral delivery, resulted in sustained synaptic protection in 5XFAD mice and throughout the course of prion disease, preventing behavioural deficits and neuronal loss and significantly prolonging survival. In contrast, knockdown of RBM3 exacerbated synapse loss in both models and accelerated disease and prevented the neuroprotective effects of cooling. Thus, deficient synapse regeneration, mediated at least in part by failure of the RBM3 stress response, contributes to synapse loss throughout the course of neurodegenerative disease. The data support enhancing cold-shock pathways as potential protective therapies in neurodegenerative disorders.

[http://www.sciencealert.com/scientists-discover-cold-shock-proteins-for-dementia-treatment](http://www.sciencealert.com/scientists-discover-cold-shock-proteins-for-dementia-treatment)

# Thread: Molecular Hydrogen/H2 - Ultimate Mitochondrial/Redox Compound?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/molecular-hydrogen-h2-ultimate-mitochondrial-redox-compound.12674/>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 45
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### bio-fractal-soul-self — 2015-01-23T08:40:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/molecular-hydrogen-h2-ultimate-mitochondrial-redox-compound.12674/post-153668>

Hey there everyone - hope all is well! Have yet to make an intro post, but will in due time. Anyhow, before I get to doing a bit of work, I wanted to post about something that has been subtly mentioned before, though in different molecular forms and in a different context - hydrogen.

Hydrogen is very interesting. As I continue my journey studying science - and recovering my health - I plan on incorporating a lot more physics and chemistry into my curriculum so I can more readily grasp these things, make _novel_ connections, etc. to help more people. Anyhow, since I've got some work to do, I'm going to just post some studies and what I find interesting, perhaps I can elaborate more later, and begin to ask everyone here for any available input.

**[Molecular hydrogen as a preventive and therapeutic medical gas: initiation, development and potential of hydrogen medicine](http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0163725814000941)**

"Molecular hydrogen (H2) has been accepted to be an inert and nonfunctional molecule in our body. We have turned this concept by demonstrating that H2 reacts with strong oxidants such as hydroxyl radical in cells, and proposed its potential for preventive and therapeutic applications. H2 has a number of advantages exhibiting extensive effects: H2 rapidly diffuses into tissues and cells, _and it is mild enough neither to disturb metabolic redox reactions nor to affect signaling reactive oxygen species;_ therefore, there should be no or little adverse effects of H2. There are several methods to ingest or consume H2; inhaling H2gas, drinking H2-dissolved water (H2-water), injecting H2-dissolved saline (H2-saline), taking an H2 bath, or dropping H2-saline into the eyes. **The numerous publications on its biological and medical benefits revealed that H2 reduces oxidative stress not only by direct reactions with strong oxidants, but also indirectly by regulating various gene expressions. Moreover, by regulating the gene expressions, H2functions as an anti-inflammatory and anti-apoptotic, and stimulates energy metabolism.** In addition to growing evidence obtained by model animal experiments, extensive clinical examinations were performed or are under investigation. Since most drugs specifically act to their targets, H2 seems to differ from conventional pharmaceutical drugs. Owing to its great efficacy and lack of adverse effects, H2 has promising potential for clinical use against many diseases."

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### bio-fractal-soul-self — 2015-01-23T08:43:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/molecular-hydrogen-h2-ultimate-mitochondrial-redox-compound.12674/post-153669>

**[Recent Progress Toward Hydrogen Medicine: Potential of Molecular Hydrogen for Preventive and Therapeutic Applications](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3257754/#!po=30.2632)** [](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3257754/figure/F1/?report=objectonly)

"Persistent oxidative stress is one of the major causes of most lifestyle-related diseases, cancer and the aging process. Acute oxidative stress directly causes serious damage to tissues. Despite the clinical importance of oxidative damage, antioxidants have been of limited therapeutic success. We have proposed that molecular hydrogen (H2) has potential as a “novel” antioxidant in preventive and therapeutic applications \[Ohsawa_et al_., Nat Med. 2007: 13; 688-94\]. H2 has a number of advantages as a potential antioxidant: H2 rapidly diffuses into tissues and cells, and it is mild enough neither to disturb metabolic redox reactions nor to affect reactive oxygen species (ROS) that function in cell signaling, thereby, there should be little adverse effects of consuming H2. **There are several methods to ingest or consume H2, including inhaling hydrogen gas, drinking H2-dissolved water (hydrogenwater), taking a hydrogen bath, injecting H2-dissolved saline (hydrogen saline), dropping hydrogen saline onto the eye, and increasing the production of intestinal H2 by bacteria.** Since the publication of the first H2paper in _Nature Medicine_ in 2007, the biological effects of H2 have been confirmed by the publication of more than 38 diseases, physiological states and clinical tests in leading biological/medical journals, and several groups have started clinical examinations. **Moreover, H2 shows not only effects against oxidative stress, but also various anti-inflammatory and anti-allergic effects.** H2 regulates various gene expressions and protein-phosphorylations, though the molecular mechanisms underlying the marked effects of very small amounts of H2 remain elusive.""Despite their cytotoxic effects, low concentrations of ROS, such as ●O2- and H2O2, function as signaling molecules and regulate apoptosis, cell proliferation, and differentiation \[[14](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3257754/#R14), [15](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3257754/#R15)\]. As mentioned, unexpectedly and notably, recent studies have suggested that excessive antioxidants increased mortality and rates of cancer \[[9](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3257754/#R9), [11](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3257754/#R11), 59-62\] because they may interfere with some essential defensive mechanisms \[[13](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3257754/#R13), [60](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3257754/#R60),[63](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3257754/#R63)\-[67](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3257754/#R67)\]. At higher concentrations, H2O2 is converted to hypochlorous acid by myeloperoxidase to defend against bacterial invasion \[[68](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3257754/#R68)\]. Additionally, NO functions as a neurotransmitter and is essential for the dilation of blood vessels \[[69](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3257754/#R69)\].Since H2 reduces ●OH but does not affect ●O2- and H2O2 having physiological roles \[[12](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3257754/#R12)\], we propose that the adverse effects of H2 are very small compared to other antioxidants."

"Hydrogen water can be made by several methods, including dissolving hydrogen gas in water under high pressure, dissolving electrolyzed H2 in water, and by the reaction of magnesium metal with water. The method of dissolving hydrogen gas under high pressure has an advantage because it is applicable not only using water but also any other solvents."

"In Parkinson’s disease, mitochondrial dysfunction and the associated oxidative stress are major causes of dopaminergic cell loss in the substantia nigra \[[102](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3257754/#R102)\]. H2 in drinking water was given before or after stereotactic surgery for 6-hydroxydopamine-induced nigrostrital degeneration in a rat model of Parkinson’s disease. Hydrogen water prevented both the development and progression of nigrostriatal degeneration. **Hydrogen water likely retards the development and progression of Parkinson’s disease \[[103](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3257754/#R103)\]. Drinking hydrogen water suppressed dopaminergic neuronal loss in another Parkinson’s disease model induced by MPTP (1-methyl-4-phenyl-1,2,3,6-tetrahydropyridine) \[[104](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3257754/#R104)\]."

"Increased oxidative stress in obesity affects metabolic syndrome \[[111](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3257754/#R111)\]. _Long-term drinking of hydrogen water significantly controlled fat and body weights, despite no change in the consumption of food and water._Moreover, drinking hydrogen water decreased levels of plasma glucose, insulin and triglyceride, the effect of which on hyperglycemia was similar to diet restriction \[[112](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3257754/#R112)\]. A mechanistic study revealed that the gene expression of the hepatic hormone, fibroblast growth factor 21 (FGF21) was enhanced, which should function to enhance fatty acid and glucose expenditure. Indeed, drinking hydrogen water stimulated energy metabolism, as measured by O2consumption and CO2 expiration. **These results suggest the potential benefit of H2 in improving obesity, diabetes and metabolic syndrome \[[112](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3257754/#R112)\]."****

****[Molecular hydrogen: an overview of its neurobiological effects and therapeutic potential for bipolar disorder and schizophrenia](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3680337/)****

"Molecular hydrogen is an odourless and tasteless gas that **has the ability to rapidly diffuse through lipid membranes and enter the cell, where it easily penetrates organelles such as mitochondria as well as the nucleus.** It is inert at room temperature and in the absence of catalysts. It additionally easily **crosses the blood brain barrier**, which facilitates access to the target organs and subcellular components. Its adverse event profile is reportedly benign \[[2](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3680337/#B2),[63](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3680337/#B63)\]. Physiologically, hydrogen is produced by intestinal microbiota from fermentation of complex carbohydrates. Arterial blood contains higher levels of hydrogen that tissue, suggesting some uptake and utilization of hydrogen in tissues. Hydrogen reacts with free hyrodoxyl radicals but does not appear to react to other reactive oxygen species. This is a theoretical advantage, as low levels of these radicals have physiologically relevant signaling effects. It protects against secondary oxidative damage to the brain in a variety of models by reacting with hydroxyl radicals \[[64](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3680337/#B64)\]. Hydrogen is potentially available as a medical gas,hydrogen enriched water, taking a hydrogen bath, injecting hydrogen saline, hydrogen saline eye drops, and augmenting bacterial production of intestinal hydrogen. It penetrates glass but not aluminum containers."

"Anti-inflammatory effects of hydrogen molecule have been reported in both animal and human models. Hydrogen-rich saline decreases the levels of cytokines IL-4, IL-5, IL-13 and TNF-α in bronchoalveolar lavage fluid \[[65](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3680337/#B65)\]. Its effects on tumor necrosis factor alpha, interleukin (IL)-1β and IL-6 levels is hypothesized as the reason for its protective role against UVB radiation \[[66](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3680337/#B66)\]. Hydrogen molecule also stops TNFα-induced activation of the NFκB pathway \[[67](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3680337/#B67)\]. Hydrogen released by intestinal microbiota, such a hydrogen producing strain of E. coli can suppress inflammation \[[68](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3680337/#B68)\]. Taken together, these findings are germane to bipolar disorder as current evidences implicates many interleukins in the neurobiology of bipolar disorder \[[69](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3680337/#B69)\].""**In a pilot placebo controlled, randomized, double-blind, parallel-group design (N

\=

18), the efficacy of 1000ml molecular hydrogen daily was investigated in patients taking levodopa. In the hydrogen group, there was a reduction in Total Unified Parkinson’s Disease Rating Scale scores, whereas the placebo group deteriorated \[[83](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3680337/#B83)\].** Signal modulating activities of hydrogen may play a role in exerting a protective effect against Parkinson’s \[[79](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3680337/#B79)\] (...) A randomized controlled trial indicated that hydrogen-enriched water decreased mitochondrial dysfunction and inflammation in patients with mitochondrial myopathies \[[89](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3680337/#B89)\]."

### bio-fractal-soul-self — 2015-01-23T08:53:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/molecular-hydrogen-h2-ultimate-mitochondrial-redox-compound.12674/post-153670>

For anyone interested, and Jack himself, too, I'd check out the work of biochemical student with an undergraduate degree only from BYU, Tyler LeBaron at the [Molecular Hydrogen Institute](http://www.molecularhydrogeninstitute.com/). There are a bunch of studies, [here](http://www.molecularhydrogeninstitute.com/studies/).

Jack discussed using H2 in water that has been super cooled and magnetized and stored in copper prior to CT, along side raw oysters. I think this would be a super cocktail for fat loss, in addition to nootropic and hedonic benefits. I'm not sure where you can find powdered diatomic hydrogen, but there is one machine on the market that does in fact make H2 containing water, but it's really expensive.

There are also tablets that I believe use a form of magnesium that dissipates into H2 in the gut. I suspect this would provide less H2 per serving than super-saturated water, though. Turmeric and other compounds with polyphenols and prebiotics also support H2 production in the gut. Lastly, as evidenced by the above, you technically can use it in a bath, via inhalation, as an eye drop, injection, etc. but I know of no one who has used methods outside of drinking H2-containing water, and I'm not sure how to go about using these other methods anyhow. I'd be willing to try it though....

### bio-fractal-soul-self — 2015-01-23T22:26:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/molecular-hydrogen-h2-ultimate-mitochondrial-redox-compound.12674/post-153719>

**Effects of drinking hydrogen-rich water on the quality of life of patients treated with radiotherapy for liver tumors**

**[](http://www.medicalgasresearch.com/content/1/1/11/figure/F1?highres=y)**
[](http://www.medicalgasresearch.com/content/1/1/11/figure/F2?highres=y)
**"Hydrogen water mitigated oxidative stress marker during radiotherapy**. Antioxidative effects in patients with placebo water (n = 24) and hydrogen rich water (n = 25). The dROM level (A) represents the total level of peroxide metabolities, and BAP (B) reflects serum antioxidant capacity."

**[Oral ‘hydrogen water' induces neuroprotective ghrelin secretion in mice](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4070541/)**
"Our findings demonstrate that the neuroprotective effects of oral hydrogen water, which produces negligible levels of H2 in the brain, result from gastric induction of the neuroprotective peptide hormone ghrelin and the subsequent activation of ghrelin receptors. In addition, we have shown an obligate role for β1-adrenergic receptors in hydrogen water-induced ghrelin up-regulation in plasma, consistent with previous reports that adrenergic stimulation regulates ghrelin release _in vitro_ and _in vivo_[22](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4070541/#b22),[24](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4070541/#b24),[25](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4070541/#b25).
(...)
These results emphasize the importance of gastric ghrelin induction in the neuroprotective action of H2. Oral H2-water is being explored as a therapeutic for PD as well as a variety of other human pathophysiological conditions[17](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4070541/#b17),[32](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4070541/#b32),[33](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4070541/#b33) under the generally held assumption that the mechanism of action of supplemental H2 is likely to reflect an antioxidative role. Our findings that oral H2 water exerts a neuroprotective effect through activation of an endogenous, gastric ghrelin system that is tightly coupled to β-adrenergic receptor signaling suggests the possibility of novel applications of H2 therapy for various diseases."

META 1: damn those are some good first posts
META 2: Dr Kruse would probably tell you to put up a profile pic showing an actual human being
\---

99% of the H2 produced in the gut is in the large intestine, under high CO2 and low O2 conditions.

I don't know how consuming H2 dissolved in water will affect this, but I suspect that it will dissipate before it reaches the large intestine. I don't know how this affects H2 production down the line in the colon  (are there light-based regulatory mechanisms that sense extra H2 in the small intestine, that give feedback to colonic bacteria to reduce H2 production?)

Some random paper I found-- [http://www.nejm.org/doi/pdf/10.1056/NEJM196907172810303](http://www.nejm.org/doi/pdf/10.1056/NEJM196907172810303) , suggesting that:

-   "Respiratory H2 excretion can be used as an indicator of intestinal H2 production".

-   "small-bowel production was increased in a patient with excessive numbers of small-bowel bacteria". SIBO + Candida is not good

-   **H2 production in man is primarily dependent upon the delivery of ingested, fermentable substrates to an abundant intestinal flora that normally is present only in the colon**

High butyrate production is likely coupled to H2 production.

Eat lots of animal fibre (prawn shells, tough beef tendons, etc ....) . See -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.ph...ntial-through-hydride-ions.10343/#post-118768](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/do-raw-foods-improve-redox-potential-through-hydride-ions.10343/#post-118768) and [http://caloriesproper.com/animal-fibre/](http://caloriesproper.com/animal-fibre/)

Another paper -- [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC442992/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC442992/)

We conclude that most H2 produced by colonic bacteria is consumed and methanogenesis and fecal stirring (via its influence on fecal PH2) are critical determinants of H2 consumption and, hence, net H2 production​

### Jack Kruse — 2015-02-04T23:19:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/molecular-hydrogen-h2-ultimate-mitochondrial-redox-compound.12674/post-154741>

When hydrogen is ionized or charge separated………however, what can happen in life at the cell level changes in a big way……….hydrogen becomes the superman of flow. When hydrogen is ionized and loses its only electron it becomes a proton cation. This makes H+ the lightest cation in chemistry and given small size of the proton, explains the unusually high diffusion rate of the proton relative to that of other common cations like potassium (K+). When hydrogen loses its electron is becomes an ionic plasma that acts like a liquid metal. Ionic plasma’s have special abilities. One ability is called proton jump conduction or protonicity. These rules are governed by something called the [Grotthuss mechanism.](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Grotthuss_mechanism) Your gut microbiome should make 10-12 L of H2 a day.......no one does in this modern world where the hydroxyl free radical is made in ridiculous amounts by nn EMF

[Origin of microbial life hypothesis: a gel cytoplasm lacking a bilayer membrane, with infrared radiation producing exclusion zone (EZ) water, hydrogen as an energy source and thermosynthesis for bioenergetics.](<http://Origin of microbial life hypothesis: a gel cytoplasm lacking a bilayer membrane, with infrared radiation producing exclusion zone \(EZ\) water, hydrogen as an energy source and thermosynthesis for bioenergetics.>) (research done by Gerald Pollack and J.T. Trevors)

Not sure if the above link is going to work, I'll take it down if not and try another way to post it.

**(Edit: the link didn't work; go to libgen (unless you have access at a local college or your university) and type in the exact title above when looking for 'scientific articles'/studies, and you'll find it and can download it as a pdf)**

Anyhow, I feel as though this may be related to what you discuss above, Dr. Kruse. They discuss how the first prebiotic bacterial cells didn't have a fatty bilayer, but due to IR radiation, developed a gel-like 'cell wall' with an increasingly large and negatively charged EZ (the negative charge extended slightly past the gel-like 'wall' too). Additionally, hydrogen could diffuse through this, building up and moving about in the 'cytoplasm', as an early Earth cellular energy source. Now, if we look at Fig. 1 in the document, with the fact that hydrogen diffuses through this gel-like membrane, couldn't it build up in the 'inner-membrane' where it acts like ionic plasma, donating itself (very rapidly) to the different oxygen molecules in the water that forms the base of the gel-like membrane, via this Grotthuss Mechanism? It would then be the cellular 'universal energy' that Prof. Pollack and his colleague Trevors describe, and it would fuel the cytoplasms movement, gel-like cell wall attraction to other things, etc. and basically be the fuel for the evolution that IR energy is causing.

I could be wayyy off base here, hence why as I keep saying, I need to up my physics+chemistry coursework lol. Regardless, this would kind of make sense especially in light of Dr. Kruse leaving those little breadcrumbs of the gut microbiome being a 'light and hydrogen story'. Perhaps infrared energy to our gut could alter our microbiome, huh?

### bio-fractal-soul-self — 2015-02-05T00:22:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/molecular-hydrogen-h2-ultimate-mitochondrial-redox-compound.12674/post-154751>

"Life is all about charge separation. In photosynthesis we use sunlight to separated charges. **In animals we use hydrophillic semiconductors to do the same thing. What is the fluid of charge separation? Water. Water next to a hydrophilic semiconductor acts like a N type semiconductor. This makes the water adjacent next to the semiconductor negative.** The further you go away from it the charge is positive. What helps energize this simple battery? More sunlight.........its energizes can alter that battery function positively or negatively. In the zone next to the semiconductor Becker found something called the **DC current.** This is a negatively charged current. He had no idea where it came from. Pauling, Ling, and Szent Georgyi did. It **came from the separation of water into hydrogen oxygen and electrons.** In the exclusion zone of water physicists like Mae Wan Ho have showed electrons carry the charge. **It also turns out these electrons reside in oxygen molecules in the water. They are usually distributed throughout the water lattice adjacent to the hydrophilic semiconductor.** The number of oxygen atoms dictates the DC current. The larger oxygen content the larger the electron density. This is why cold water water carries more Oxygen. It is more electron dense because cold water has more dissolved o2 in it........This is at the core of why CT is powerful. It allows for more electrons to be dissolved in the major fluid of your cellular matrix to deliver more energy by liberation of these electrons when charge separation happens....................This is where temperature sculpts. Physics.......and physical chemistry showing you why chemistry and biology left quite a few lessons out of their bags of tricks. Water chemistry is the biggy they forgot." - Dr. Kruse

Have a lot to think about now lol.

Maybe youy just overdid it? Maybe it was kind of a detox? IDK for sure....... I would increase the amounts slowly... with some breaks in between I think... that your gut has time to adjust...

Inger, do you ever measure your blood glucose or blood ketones? I'm interested because I know that when you're eating essentially entirely raw, especially when also doing CT, you seem to be able to 'tolerate' a much larger amount of food/a lot more calories, but this would also come with a ton more protein, essentially knocking one out of ketosis. Now, this was something I've been meaning to ask @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) - perhaps when eating epi-paleo and primarily raw, full-blown hyperketonemia is not actually what we're shooting for. For instance, the Inuit according to a large body of research did NOT eat a ketogenic diet, they ate a moderately high to high fat + high protein diet; one study I found via Lucas Tafur (who Jack used to cite) showed that one inuit sect ate about 220 grams of protein a day.

So, I'm thinking that epi-paleo+CT+most foods raw, especially raw oysters, eggs, etc. may negate the whole need for chronic hyperketonemia, and the whole protein+calorie restriction argument by many in the CRON circle - and even some in the keto proponent circle (Rosedale and some others) - falls flat on its face. I mean, just thinking about it from a non-scientific point of view, our pre-human ancestors who may have eaten a raw epi-paleo diet during the period of rapid brain encephalization, they probably weren't looking at an abundance of shell fish and thinking "okay, I can only eat 3 more oz's because otherwise I'll have eaten 20g of protein too much and won't have elevated BHB levels"; in fact, they probably ate a lottt of this stuff to build a brain that 'became human'. I've also noticed that Jack frequently talks about eating like 3 dozen oysters, tons of rare/raw steak, etc. which is already way too much protein to be in ketosis.

When a DC current is passed though a water solution, water is oxidized at the anode producing O2 and reduced at the cathode producing H2. The solution becomes acidic at the anode and basic at the cathode. At the anode: 2 H2O (l) = O2 (g) + 4 H+ (aq) + 4 e- (indicator blue) Acidic pH. At the cathode: 4 H2O (l) + 4 e- = 2 H2 (g) + 4 OH- (aq) (indicator yellow) alkaline pH

### kyrakitty — 2017-03-11T04:33:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/molecular-hydrogen-h2-ultimate-mitochondrial-redox-compound.12674/post-210094>

Here you go. DIY hydrogen water that is super cheap:

I am going to do another version, that was inspired by the video above, but it doesn't use boiling water or glass bottles. Too easy for them to shatter. People on Longecity forum have had good success using the below mentioned version. Both versions are discussed in detail at this [blog](http://pieeconomics.blogspot.com/p/blog-page_2.html) and has links for where to get the materials needed. I have everything except the corks for the test tube. But I am going to try this:

**Hydrogen Water Test tube method**

1\. First use a funnel to fill the bottle with filtered water up to about 2 inches from the top. This partial filling of the bottle helps cushion the fall as you drop the stoppered test tube, containing the magnesium rod, into the bottle.

2\. Put one gram, or slightly less than one-quarter teaspoon of malic acid powder into the test tube.

3\. Place magnesium rod into the test tube.

4\. Using the funnel add only a small amount of filtered water to the test tube, so that the liquid level is about an inch lower than the top of the test tube.

5\. Twist the stopper gently but firmly until about half of the cork is inside the test tube. Caution: You must be gentle because the test tube is glass.

6\. Drop the stoppered test tube, containing the magnesium rod, into the bottle with the filtered water. The contents of the test tube are normally heavy enough to cause the test tube to sink all the way in.

7\. Then, quickly continue adding filtered water to the bottle so that it slightly overflows.

8\. Immediately screw the cap tightly onto the bottle and place into the fridge for 1 hours

9\. After an hour hold the bottle upright and shake the bottle side to side (left and right - not up and down).

NOTE: The plastic bottles are category #1 recyclables – PETE or PET (Polyethylene Terephthalate), and are generally considered safe, although you have to be careful that bacteria do not accumulate when reusing them.

NOTE: Shaking is important… According to Hip's tests, "when you don't shake the bottle, you only get 1 ppm concentration (even though the internal pressure in the bottle is 6 atmospheres); but after just 30 seconds of vigorous shaking, you get 5 ppm."

NOTE: The cork stopper needs to allow for proper venting of the hydrogen gas from the test tube, while at the same time not letting any of the water surrounding outside the test tube in. For our application the lower quality cork is appropriate, since it is desirable for bubbles to escape the test tube by working their way through irregularities in the cork. The lower XX quality cork we are using (note that all qualities are food grade) allows for venting while higher quality cork might not.

If additional venting is needed, try punching small holes vertically all the way through the middle of the cork using a pin, needle, or sharp nail. After you brew each bottle determine whether to punch more holes. If drinking water leaks through the cork into the test tube you have punched too many holes, and will have to start over using another cork.

### kyrakitty — 2017-03-13T02:04:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/molecular-hydrogen-h2-ultimate-mitochondrial-redox-compound.12674/post-210219>

I forgot to say the point of the post I made above is that the TEST TUBE method I outline **under** the video is **completely different** than how the guy is doing in the video. The test tube method only uses 1 magnesium rod and the guy in the video uses 4-5 rods in each batch. The test tube method was specifically designed to eliminate/redduce potential heavy metal contamination. How? The idea is that putting the magnesium rod into the test tube is theorized to prevent the heavy metals from getting into the hydrogen rich water.

How? As the reaction between the magnesium rod and malic acid occurs it pushes the hydrogen gas out through the small holes in the cork into the water. The bottle used is a tightly sealed water bottle made to hold pressurized liquids like carbonated spring water and it creates some sort of pressurized air barrier keeping the part that is having the reaction with the metal rod isolated inside the test tube.. meaning any potential contaminants, where the chemical reaction is occurring, primarily stays IN the test tube.

That is basically what the citizen scientists over at Longecity theorize is happening and they specifically created this new test tube method to stop any potential heavy metal contamination... BUT the only way to know for sure is to TEST!

All the materials needed are sooooo cheap (under $40) to make hundreds and hundreds of batches of molecular hydrogen rich water... It is worth testing a batch or two to see if this theory works.

Anyone know of a good reliable place to send water samples for heavy metals testing? Hydrogen isn't stable in water so they can't test for hydrogen but if there are a lot of heavy metal contaminants in the water those should be stable. If it isn't too expensive maybe I will sent a batch or two for testing and I will post the results.

The guy who made the video, where his magnesium rods and malic acid are dropped into the water and not isolated in a test tube, tested a batch and he claims it came up with no major signs of heavy metals. But when using any new magnesium rods for the first time it would be important to test each batch to be sure they are consistently free of high amounts of heavy metals.

None of these say anything about a personal phone or skype consultation. What must one do to accomplish that?

I've just been "officially" informed yesterday that I have Hashi's but I have to wait until April 15 for my endocrinologist referral. My GP will not treat it himself, and he was opposed to the compounding pharmacist's recommendation that I go on iodine drops and topical applications over my thyroid. My GP has informed me that I must just sit tight and do nothing in the meantime (in fact, he actually tried to make me PROMISE not to do anything until after my endocrinologist sees me and takes over my care, including waiting to start the BHRT that I've already waited two years to start and have been so looking forward to.)

I flew across the country to the person I felt was the best plastic surgeon in the USA when I chose to get implants back in 2010, and I'm beginning to think I may well just fly to see Dr. K. in the meantime to get this show on the road if that is a valid option.

But if I can get a consult from home without shelling multiple thousands of dollars on plane fare, etc, that would be ideal seeing as how we would have to borrow every dime, or put it all on credit for the time being.

So.. can someone please enlighten me? I cannot wait any longer, and/or will not. Time to take my life - and health - back into my own hands. Thank you!

# Thread: Approaching First Birthday
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/approaching-first-birthday.12683/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 28
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### John Caton — 2015-01-24T13:22:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/approaching-first-birthday.12683/post-153758>

My name is John Caton.

I discovered this site on March 3, 2014, in response to a search on Google seeking relief for knee pain that was close to debilitating me. After about an hour of browsing some of Dr. Kruses' posts, I saw myself. I decided that the Leptin Reset certainly would do no harm so I stepped out, somewhat on blind faith. I now consider March 3, 2014, somewhat as a rebirth. Changing my diet, especially with the introduction of DHA from bivalves, crustaceans, sardines and wild caught salmon, and the serious limitation of carbohydrates in the beginning, has remade my body composition.

I was only seeking to find relief to knee pain but I ended up with a new life. I lost pounds that I had given up ever losing. I was happy just to maintain the extra 25 I was carrying and that often had required some starvation. Otherwise, I would tack on 15-20 more easily. That 25 pounds rolled off with no effort, no hunger, no exercise. My mind has been boggled. So, March 3 is my birthday.

Approaching March 3 of 2015, I just want to extend a "Thank You" to Dr. Kruse and to the knowledgeable posters on this forum that I have followed "from afar" for this past year. So much information. So much is beyond my initial understanding, but gradually it sinks in. Thank you for letting me learn from you.

John Caton

### fitness@home — 2015-01-24T15:14:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/approaching-first-birthday.12683/post-153761>

Hi John!

Welcome and I hope you post more often. The knee pain has totally resolved?

I have learned a lot from others here too...

### John Caton — 2015-01-24T15:27:47+0200

>
> Welcome and I hope you post more often. The knee pain has totally resolved?

My knee pain was gone within about 6-8 weeks. I briefly reverted to some old eating patterns, had the pain return. I started over, knee pain went away and never returned. Christmas season was tough and I consumed more sugar than I had been consuming but I got through the season without detectable consequences. I quickly resolved to get back on track.

I thank you, in advance.

### John Caton — 2015-01-26T03:00:20+0200

>
> John, since you did the Leptin reset, you are probably mindful of sunrises. Do you get much sun otherwise?
> Do you live in a big city?

I live in the middle of Dallas-Fort Worth. That's one of my hurdles to reaching my optimal.

I have always greeted the sunrise and I watch most sunsets. But, I've always been too sensitive to summer sunlight between 10:00 - 4:00 to enjoy it much. This past summer, I spent more time in the sun and did not burn.

I've always been partial to being barefoot but only while indoors. I reintroduced myself to the joy of barefootin' outdoors. Now, that has become habitual.

### John Caton — 2015-02-02T22:50:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/approaching-first-birthday.12683/post-154553>

> [MrPinkies said:](/goto/post?id=154534)

I know of no other way to express it. The differences are such that it looks and feels like two different lives. (There may be a diagnosis for that feeling. Someone told me they make a pill for it.) Really though, it's a date I want to remember because the life prior to that date is one I never want to return to. But, I feel I'm still in transition while I figure out a way to mitigate some of the technological trappings that I've allowed to ensnare me.

### John Caton — 2015-03-03T12:47:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/approaching-first-birthday.12683/post-157263>

> [John Caton said:](/goto/post?id=153769)
>
> Thank you, Dr Kruse. Someday, somewhere, I will shake your hand in gratitude. Meanwhile, on March 3, I plan to hoist an oyster in your honor. I'll happily accept your water toast.

This is my Epi-Paleo Birthday. This morning I had raw oyster stew for breakfast and as I raised the first one to my lips, I raised it amid thoughts of continued gratitude to Dr. Kruse and to all of the knowledgeable and inspirational contributors to the forum, over the past year that I have tagged along.

I'm understanding more. About a month ago, I started to reread from the same point I started a year ago. The reread more understandable. It is an unusual, but exciting process. Reading and understanding is only a small part; implementation is the greater part. It's getting easier. You guys are a big reason why it is.

### Kristi Lambert — 2015-03-03T14:42:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/approaching-first-birthday.12683/post-157273>

Happy Epi-Paleo Birthday

### MrPinkies — 2015-03-03T20:34:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/approaching-first-birthday.12683/post-157330>

yes... Happy Epi-Paleo Birthday!!

### caroline — 2015-03-03T22:38:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/approaching-first-birthday.12683/post-157342>

What other fishie things will you have today John?
Happy Birthday .....in a way It is a very real birthday! maybe the most real you have ever had.....

### John Caton — 2015-03-04T13:00:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/approaching-first-birthday.12683/post-157406>

Thanks, all. March 3, 2014 and March 3, 2015 were altogether different days. Last year, I spent the day with my painful knees searching the web for solutions. Found this site. What a find! Same day, a year later, I remain pain-free, 20+ pounds lighter, new attitude ... I still spent much of the day re-reading a lot of the earlier stuff I read last year.

My biggest challenges are still environmental. Nighttime artificial light changes were easy to implement. Non-native EMFs are still a challenge. Working to mitigate them.

Thanks for all the encouragement.

### sjoshua — 2015-03-04T20:26:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/approaching-first-birthday.12683/post-157439>

Happy Epi-birfday!

3/1/13 was when I started cold showering, and started shifting towards a more epi-paleo diet. March is just a great time for change/spring/refreshing!

### fitness@home — 2015-03-04T21:03:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/approaching-first-birthday.12683/post-157442>

> Non-native EMFs are still a challenge. Working to mitigate them.

This one area that I will always be working on both at home and work.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-01-27T17:12:55+0200

New podcast out this AM on bluetooth. You have to understand how biology reacts to non native waves. Not vice versa [http://ow.ly/HTWqQ](http://ow.ly/HTWqQ)

### Paleodocteur — 2015-01-29T18:56:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-podcast-out-on-bluetooth.12708/post-154210>

That reminded me about a case couple weeks ago . I’m now sensitive to the point where I believe I can “feel” other’s personal thunderstorms .

But when that patient came in it was so intense and of a different “flavor”. So as she is complaining about how much she is loosing her hair and how it is falling over her Ipad at night… I’m thinking” why do I feel it is still buzzing so strong on my face ? Usualy when they left their coat and purse down the signal stops bothering me…” So she undress and I still feel it …and then, there it was: the F…ing FritBit strapped to her bra! LOL

### nonchalant — 2015-01-29T20:14:14+0200

### Butters — 2015-02-17T09:59:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-podcast-out-on-bluetooth.12708/post-155852>

It's like the best podcast ever about EMF in my view. You explained it so good with the GPS example.

In the [Bluetooth Radiation May Be More Harmful Than Cell Phone Radiation](http://inflanation.com/cell-phone-radiation/bluetooth-radiation-may-be-more-dangerous-than-cell-phone-radiation-2/) article its mentioned the himalayan salt lamps shield EMFs. Is it true? I think about buying one for my desk.

### Lahelada — 2015-02-17T15:12:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-podcast-out-on-bluetooth.12708/post-155875>

I looked into this question of "is it really off when it is off" Apparently it sleeps in most cases. My assumption based on anecdotal evidence from myself and reading user help forums it seems to be hovering when you have activated it,even if only once. To save you the hassle of having to identify yourself it reports its existence once and is then available

### Lahelada — 2015-02-17T15:19:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-podcast-out-on-bluetooth.12708/post-155876>

So next time you use an apps that has bluetooth funcionality lets say you download music it can assime you will want to use lets say a bluetooth headset and go into active standby. So the activity level of your bluetooth is directly related to your bluetooth capable apps on your device.
A good way to check is looking what uses your battery. As you probably know how much you use Wifi compare it to this percentage. If they are similar ur higher then you can conclude that your standby exposure is really high.
Note I have not got any studies on this but reading fault reports that seems to be the common theme.

# Thread: The quality of our relationship:
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quality-of-our-relationship.12710/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 93
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2015-01-27T21:16:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quality-of-our-relationship.12710/post-154018>

On a scale of 1 to 10, how would you rate the quality of our relationship based upon the service of this product during the last 2 years ?

One being excellent and 10 being dreadful.

### MrPinkies — 2015-01-27T21:28:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quality-of-our-relationship.12710/post-154019>

service of this product- meaning the forums? and... last week 2 years means??

In general, Jack, you and the forum has been a life saver.. I am forever grateful, and you know that... I am, also, amazed by how much time and energy you have spent creating this phenomenal community; answering questions, etc.. and most people I have met I would consider my own tribe of weirdos (aka family).. Feels nice to feel the open arms welcome and love..

Look at the goal, and work backward to create it

### rgp888 — 2015-02-10T01:06:14+0200

1

### dantothep — 2015-02-10T16:05:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quality-of-our-relationship.12710/post-155226>

In all honesty. I would give it a 4.

Like others have said the relationships and compassionate nature is a 1.

IMO what it needs is an information overhaul, not on the why but the how.

The why works well having different info nuggets here there and everywhere, Jacks mysterious one line answers encourage you to search.

The how I agree with Marie entirely, I have found myself trying to look in about 10 different places just to workout what needs to be done, by whom (context) and when, only to then read newer blog post / forum post that contradicts or has extra nuggets. For example i read the other day that the order of Rx's should be Leptin (circadian rhythms), Epi-Paleo, CT then others. I also found that there are more than one way to test for LS / LR. It took over a week to work our which lab tests are best Shinjins post was the best 3 tiers dependant on available money resources.

Then I find myself wasting time looking for a nugget of info on how which I had read before instead of reading the why.

Fixes:

1\. Intro page for newbies - well designed, with infographic. The quilt is good, but to any newbie is enough overload to make them leave.
2\. Collation of disparate information (wiki idea was good) and not just links (although links would help) for all protocols. This would help sell the paid for services too.
3\. Flow charts to asses context for the individual e.g. environment, activity levels, which protocols to look at first.
4\. Templates for journals.
5\. Certain webinars should be cheaper, I know a few forum owners who have made videos less than $10 USD, and have made far more $ than people with high price points.
6\. This is essentially online learning, so why not set certain milestones (and even small tests to ensure knowledge is captured)? That way people who are learning similar things at similar times can converse. If you are not up-to-date with the latest blog post it is easy to feel excluded.
7\. Weekly roundup's of key forum news / info nuggets for the time strapped individuals.

All these points have pro's/con's. If you did this then it would open it up to more people than the very patient / desperate, and help it to become the mainstream site that it needs to be, and get the info out there.

To summarise you need to reduce the entropy of the HOW as it is majorly inflamed.

### Penny — 2015-02-10T18:10:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quality-of-our-relationship.12710/post-155244>

For starters, have an acronym list at the beginning of each blog post saying what all those acronyms you use mean...
You still have edit problems in your post - some so bad it truly makes no sense - some of the sentences... aren't - at least a spell checker...
You can get your point across at various levels - some of us are not rocket scientists - I find myself reading other people just to attempt to fathom what you are saying - and they say it more simply it seems -
I am referring to someone like Jerry Tennant or Bruce Lipton - plus they use lots of pictures -
Jerry Tennant took about 6 pages and explained what a semiconductor was and a few other electrical concepts so we could understand -
Also, you might have little pop ups when one comes to a term like "magnetic moment" with a quick definition of what that meant - kind of like a box that overlays the words when the cursor hovers over it - my kid's online school does that -
Heck, you might also want to check out a few [www.k12.com](http://www.k12.com) sample lessons and see how easily they convey information - and how well they use the media -
[http://www.k12.com/take-a-peek/sample-lessons#.VNotwvnF9q8](http://www.k12.com/take-a-peek/sample-lessons#.VNotwvnF9q8)
The blog at this point is so unwieldy, I frankly can't wait for the book...
I liked the blog index when it was by date, as that is the order by which the original people on this forum learned - at least make it an option again -
Some of your posts are so long - if you find yourself writing long messages, maybe animations or pictures would be better?
There is so much background required to understand this stuff, I think you have an unenviable task - I mean really, people drop electrical terms, biochemical terms, medical terms all over the place and unless you've been down several rabbit holes prior to this one, it might be difficult to comprehend -
I tell you what though - I await every blog like it's Christmas and you are a helluva nice guy to be taking the time and effort to help us - and the people on this forum are really helpful and sweet and kind - weirdly, it's almost like a family - quite a feather in your cap in the grand scheme of things -
As far as technical problems go, being a software engineer, I think your staff does quite well
And thanks for being there for us - I know my life has totally changed given what I now know - well done Dr. K

### sjoshua — 2015-02-10T23:00:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quality-of-our-relationship.12710/post-155279>

1 for the information: this is a priceless compilation between the blogs & forums

1 for the time and effort you put into the blog and forums: I find it incredible that you ACTIVELY and DAILY participate in your forum, even in the silly questions sometimes. In my experience, this is unrivaled by the 'heads' such as yourself.

3 for the blogs (regarding style, formatting, readability): I often notice spelling and grammatical errors, and the format of how concepts are presented can seem to 'flow' out of order, esp. with the inclusions of the various 'geek' vs 'norm' explanations. I believe all of the content within them is invaluable, but the small errors and somewhat difficult flow can distract from the validity of the information presented.
As you have been asking for HOWs on the above comments, may I suggest:
\- a sidekick/editor who is of similar understandings as yourself with this information, but would be capable of improving 'flow' and the little fixes
\- a 'cliff notes' version of each blog with only the important points that could be accessed and absorbed in <1 minute. Similar to your 'readers summary' at the beginning, but instead not QUESTIONS but the ANSWERS, extracted in such a way that if a reader was willing to trust the information presented in short order they could, OR if they want the details and cites, they may dive into the full version. I think this could be helpful two-fold, since it would also be a quick reference for those that read the detail, should they go back and review.
\- alternatively, maybe you'd like to keep your format as is, but an outside style - such as the wiki - that presents the information in a different format may be beneficial. People learn in different ways, so having multiple facets of delivering equivalent information could prove helpful

3 for the forums style and Jack's (and other members') interaction with it: I must emphasize how grateful I am for your diligent participation in the forums, thank you again. But some nuances I've noticed:
\- Jack (and many others) will sometimes refer to people by their first names, example: telling 'newbie' to search for 'Gretch's' post on this for their answer. It is great to point out that another member may be of assistance, but there has to be a way to connect this information.
HOW: May I suggest using forum names for universal understanding of who is being addressed in the virtual medium (and even the built-in shortcut that let's us alert them such as @[sjoshua](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/5781/))
\- Jack (and others) will sometimes say it was already answered by him in a different thread.
HOW: May I suggest linking to the thread you are referring to, or even better the exact post? Otherwise, it can be difficult to navigate to something that the questioner has not seen nor knows what it is titled, the forum it appears in, etc.
\- This is slightly off-topic, but refers to forums: The 'Ask Jack' forum only allows paying members to post. I support your intent to only answer direct paying member questions here, but this causes/restricts non-members from even participating in these discussions. There have been occasions where I have come across questions there that I believe I could have provided valuable feedback to, but since I am restricted from posting I have had to resort to NOT replying, or alternatively contacting the person individually through 'Conversations'. The drawback there is, I may be able to help that individual with their situation/question, but it is no longer public for the guest/non-/members alike to view as well. Here is an example of a thread there that may be better suited in a full participation forum section: [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.ph...nsegrity-7-black-hole-sun-here-we-come.12769/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/question-where-is-the-discussions-for-post-tensegrity-7-black-hole-sun-here-we-come.12769/)
HOW: May I suggest if feasible, allowing non-members to REPLY to the 'Ask Jack' forums, but merely not post new questions?
\- EDIT: another thought regarding the forums, right now there are many blog/webinar/Q&A discussion threads, and it is typical that they are started in various forums.
HOW: May I suggest, setting up a forum header for EACH of the specific type: i.e., have a forum section that is for blog/webinar/Q&A, with a subforum within each of these that speaks to them directly. Then, within the BLOG subforum, for example, every blog would get a discussion thread, etc. for the other types. This would make it much more efficient to find those discussions.

3 for the format in which information is presented: Jack, you posses a conductors knowledge of the orchestra, per se. My understanding is that your style attempts to spoon feed concepts and ideas, which I think is a great way for some to learn, but since some concepts are occasionally addressed to different scopes in different blogs that can be separated by months or even years, without the readers being conductors as well, it can be difficult to fully construct the puzzle. I realize your blog index separates some of this information, but for example, if I scroll down to 'Autism' and then click on 'Brain Gut 13', I am presented with 15 pages of reading and Autism is only mentioned once throughout.
HOW, may I suggest:
\-A more in-depth blog index, inclusive of the ability to link to the specific paragraphs that the reader's relevant search is about instead of the blog in entirety.
\- Again, I think an outside wiki-style page that addressed a specific concept/point, and then proceeded to link to the specific instances that concept/point was discussed in every single blog it appears.

3 for newbies: The 'Getting Started Guide' could use some work.
HOW, may I suggest:
\- The 'Where do I start?' section could greatly utilize a more in-depth index reference or outside wiki-like page reference to help, as mentioned above
\- The 'Dr. Kruse' Protocols' section is a great reference, if the readers know what they are trying to accomplish. Some method of getting them to the point of KNOWING which protocols would benefit them individually in a more streamlined manner would be beneficial. Right now, my interpretation is they are told in the 'How Do I Absorb All This Information?' section to merely dive into the blogs... Jack you have roughly 100 blogs, I'd imagine unless very desperate, very bored, or very quick to learn your style, this would overwhelm most readers before even determining their 'need'. I think a quick reference guide and the 'cliff notes' blogs versions could expedite this.
\*aside, the 'Change Rx' and 'Concussion Rx' links both go to the same place.
\- The 'How do I use JackKruse.com' video, it may just be me, or current technical glitches, but this does not even show up as a video...

3 for membership: In your getting started guide, it is suggested individuals strongly consider the membership for faster progress, since it allows access to more information - Q&A's, webinars. As said above, 'more information' may just be additionally overwhelming considering there wasn't great direction in getting started aside from directing the reader to a huge amount of reading material... I think the membership is an excellent idea, but the method with which it is presented may not be the best way to convince people to open their wallets.
HOW, may I suggest:
\- Have you considered offering a free or 'moneyback' trial for 30/60/90 days?
\- I doubt you'd follow this idea, but I am a strong believer that the best things in life ARE free. What if membership were donation based, as to scale to the budgets of all. You have many young people on here - some teenagers! And as you are quite aware, age of disease onset is only getting younger... Imagining that they could/should/would be able to pay a monthly membership may be unrealistic.

Thanks again DR!

### PaulG — 2015-02-11T01:59:53+0200

I heartily agree with the many excellent suggestions.

I like the idea of a separate encyclopaedia/wiki site which condenses and summaries the detail into the essential and is more focused on actionable steps as opposed to the theory. If such a site could be less wordy and more visual use mind maps, flow charts perhaps that would be good.

As to improving the forum there are a whole bunch of add-ons (1,561) that could be useful - just need someone from the team to review them
[https://xenforo.com/community/resources/categories/add-ons.28/](https://xenforo.com/community/resources/categories/add-ons.28/)

For example I am not a fan of Likes as to me it adds little value and clutters the Recent Activity Feed, so my suggestion would be to turn off likes and implement a tagging add-on that would allow moderators to categorize important threads and messages (like panning for gold)

### patgrif@hotmail.com — 2015-03-06T05:00:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quality-of-our-relationship.12710/post-157605>

Dr Kruse, I don't know if you are definitely coming to LA around the middle of the month..............but if you are I know of this 'perfect' (I think) restaurant for you. It's by the ocean south of Malibu (Topanga actually) it's called "The Reel Inn" it's a fish place (pun on reel) and I really like their oysters. I am not an oyster connoisseur but I much prefer them to the Whole Foods ones. To-day they had something 'bragging' about them they are called Kumiai Oysters and they get them from Baja California. They have a big menu of fish (they do not serve any meats etc) and they also have wine.

So far so good right? But it's even better than that there is a 'river' as it would be called here in Ireland we would call it a 'stream' right behind the restaurant. Anyway it's flowing pretty good this time of the year and 3 days this week I have had really nice CT sessions there by myself, it is totally 'isolated' hardly ever see a person there. I have dug out the stream a bit so the water completely covers me. For some reason I much prefer it now to the ocean for CT I mean, I even drink the water too. It's more 'peaceful' than the ocean and there are trees there and I love it all the sound of the river, the cold all of it. I don't really expect you to do this CT there I mean but I thought if was 'funny' like everything in one place

I also want you to know it would be my 'treat'.........a small token for the over 3 years of intellectual excitement and energy. But even better not just 'intellectual' but a really USEFUL thing that has made quite a difference in my life. I really hope you are coming. It's also fine of course if other people want to come along though I kind of like the idea of having your un-divided attention

### smart cat — 2015-03-12T14:25:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quality-of-our-relationship.12710/post-158348>

Your Blog, Forum, Podcasts were the diamonds that I needed to find to even have a chance to get well. Back then in 2011 I listened to Jimmy Moore's podcast and the rest is history. Finally I found the doctor I was looking for.
Unfortunately not in my area in Germany.
And I didn't have any money for a doctor's visit in Nashville or skype/E-mail-Consults. Later I had money but there were no internet consults any more. Shit happens.

Most of what I think has been already written in this thread , so I keep it short:

**Ratings:**
Jack Kruse
the person, the physician with a mission and the most helpful knowlege I ever found since searching the web in 2009 or so = **"1"**

The people in the forum
Very smart people, friendly and supporting atmosphere, no trolls. I feel I fit in better than in every other forum I have been (think German _Paleo_ Facebook Groups or Physical Culture related _Fighter Fightness Forum_ = **"1"**

The blog contents
Information delivered = **"2"** (only subjective -the importance for me, not for others)
Technical aspects, digestibility for non- geeks, clarity, structure has a lot of room for improvement = **"5"**

You could put the most common and repeated questions plus answers in there and it might save you a lot of time - and the moderators could refer people to it - you could also update it periodically -

### patgrif@hotmail.com — 2015-03-15T18:06:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quality-of-our-relationship.12710/post-158560>

I just woke up around 9 am now................was at Jack's speech and more to the point this several Q@A's afterwards left around 1am. So I have actually got close to 8 hours sleep but I feel DESTROYED! Really quite as bad as some of my college days but those involved some very heavy drinking plus maybe some pot...........depending. Anyway yesterday I had no alcohol no 'abuse' of food even drank plenty of water but just the fact of being up so late (I usually go to bed around 8-9 pm) but honestly that feels 'tied' with the effect of the lights. Actually I would say the effect of the lights feels more (worse)

Big hanging chandeliers in the lecture room, I mean like a about 6 MASSIVE chandeliers and they weren't even turned all the way up a few times they were and it felt BAD, like this can really destroy the brain. I suppose it means my 'light hygiene' is quite good by comparison I am not at all used to this. So I can believe and it was an object lesson in Jack's theories about light, I feel right now like I have had about 20 cheese cakes.

The other thing is 'watching' Jack.................I have no idea how he can do it. The talk the questions being in the firing line he kept going and going and going and he was still going when I gave up at 1am. Overall I think it went quite well, I think it was a difficult assignment for Jack in that the audience was hard to get a grip on. They were very varied but mostly 'entrepreneurial' types but also a lot of guys with knowledge of physics (tech people) but not of biology much. Which of course was find as Jack was mostly saying how physics trumps biology so in that way he got a decent hearing I would say. It was hard for me to judge I have heard most of it in one way or another already but I was impressed by some of the questioners. They clearly are 'smart' people and have been successful thinking for themselves and not being 'push overs'

Jack should be on a plane by now.................I have to admit I cannot conceive how he does it his 'redox' must be about 10 times mine. I need to lie down now and maybe get some more sleep or at least rest.................
