### Paleodocteur — 2017-04-10T21:24:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-212121>

Haden’t been there in a while
My colleagues go « wow you got tanned over there! but I ‘m thinking it’s my MSH going on overdrive during the train ride"

I had to give a speech on hormonal development …and how light would affect it.
I was nervous but thought I could mitigate, stayed fasted for 48h, grounded, cold, hydrated.
felt good but nervousness increased when noticed the ceiling was laden with old CFLs spots
Quickly my skin turned pink an dry , HR in the hundreds
As for the speech,I was not very articulate ( blame the frying light and RF mic or my shyness )
but I think colleagues got the picture.

### Paleodocteur — 2017-06-29T20:46:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-217501>

Summer Solstice labs after 10 days of sunrise gazing
lost 4 pounds
Hb 15,4g/dl
ESR 5
urea low down to 0,15g/l
creat 9mg/l unchanged
FBS 0,87g/l HbA1C 5,2% stable
trig 0,6 g/l chol 2,5 HDL 0,9
K+4,4 unchanged
hs CRP 1,5 (ouch but down from 40 in april )
testo 4,6 midrange (down from a year ago5,2)
S-DHEA 386 (down from a year ago 405 ) still in upper quartile
unsupplemented vitD 39 ng/ml ( descent midday sun grazing, down from49 ng/ml post winter tropical refill)

### Jack Kruse — 2017-06-29T20:57:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-217506>

Nice.....no new paintings?

### Paleodocteur — 2017-06-29T21:50:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-217509>

Not much and that bugs me ....

### Paleodocteur — 2017-06-29T21:55:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-217511>

If I didn't know better I'd say grain brain

Not optimal labs, far from optimal food choices for the last 5 months , cravings .

mirror test : middle age man, hair getting whiter, stronger biceps , wider shoulders, more days with moobs than without, more fat on upper belly.
Mood: weather dependent but luckily mostly sunny here so I get good morning rays.
I still tank when atmospheric pressure drops.

But I feel much more resilient than during the equinox.
Late afternoon population movement and cell tower peaks seems to be a major driver for cravings , not so in the morning when my electron flow is still steady.

EMFSensitivity is varying but seems less than before or I am becoming more indifferent to it ( whatever it does to me, i find it is VERY unhealthy to freak out each time someone is zapping me)
Sleep: solid, mostly spontaneous wake up with first photons peaking through the blinds (or right as the street lamps shut down). Doesn’t happen when weather gets gloomy but it’s hard to know if that is due to delayed photonic rise or uneven nightly repair because of stormy weather. When I wake up and am able to catch surya rising I feel great, and the day feels MUCH longer , by that I mean longer than the extra awake hour it allows. Qualitatively longer.

Creativity: down . less creativity for about a year . very few drawings, correlates with weight gains, correlate with the move to this « better" house. Was frying my skin in the previous one but at least my drive was much stronger. I guess artists need to to be slightly tortured to produce something.

Cognition: down . I still print blogs, cites, and "cited by » articles, hundreds of abstracts a month. I find some great books. But its becoming increasingly hard to commit to read them all. It feels like my "hard drive" is full and adding another piece to the puzzle only adds to the clutter…

### Paleodocteur — 2017-07-30T17:48:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-220445>

Is sunrise flickering ?
That was my impression a few days ago , when I'd stare at the disc it was shimmering
about ten times per second....

### Paleodocteur — 2017-07-30T19:09:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-220458>

Now if I imagine a sailor filled with electrons, going back to Hamburg. Is he going to have an acute rise or drop of dopamine ? Is it going to last? Will it be the same if he flew back home instead of a monthly journey?

In a way I already experienced that twenty years ago when I came back from a three year stay on the equator to a cold wet northern harbor. I was happy, filled with energy, wanted to have talk and party . But by the end of winter energy seemed to have receded and I ("We") badly needed a sun refill.

### Paleodocteur — 2017-07-30T19:39:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-220461>

So there was a prolonged high
but then was it because my UV reserves had been refilled to the rim and enabled me to interact or because my HPA axis was on overdrive to compensate for UV deficit?

### Paleodocteur — 2017-08-03T20:37:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-220849>

SNAP
hackbump
For four years I have been trying to ground regularly with some good effects on my posture, my chicken calves grew , my soles are getting more leathery. I feel better resilience to weather changes.. (I also got minor cuts, spindles, glass, dogs, beestings, but that’s peanut…)
3 month ago I wanted to up it from about 1 hour a day to about 3 hours a day. To do that I decided to walk barefoot as much as possible. I received a new pair of minetonkas of which I removed the insole last week. since side walks and streets are petrol based I try to walk on the grass, and curbs.
With makes for a funny walk as if the front part of my feet had to adapt to a new function.. I was getting more confortable with my prehension and confident I was strengthening my tensegrity.
Monday after such a walk back home I did a minor jump of joy and fell in pain to the ground.

I snapped some fibers in my calf. no big deal . makeshift IR and icing has decreased my limping already. .

### Paleodocteur — 2017-08-03T20:39:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-220850>

### Paleodocteur — 2017-08-03T21:35:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-220864>

It’s vacation time ( 5 weeks in the sun make for a good chunk of the french paradox). This town population density is at it’s lowest. Except for downtown and the beach , laden with foreign tourists and their cellphonecamera. ( Both places I spent time before the rupture. ) . Now adda small heat wave prior to that change in microwaving landscape and it may require an overadaptation and that may be be taxing to triple helices...

### Paleodocteur — 2017-08-25T13:37:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-222225>

10 day vacation
Drove the old convertible south to the 44th to grab last bits of purple Bees
Nice lakes, pine trees, more O2. Long sand beaches, but tight sand spaces for climatic refugees (NL,B,D, UK…) clumped in small pockets of ‘safe beach’ . Beware of waves between the two flags of the swim guards. Waves are strong and current treacherous..
It seems that sand is a reflector for nonnative waves > and higher local population density > less O2, > first sun burns of the year.

### Paleodocteur — 2017-08-25T13:38:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-222226>

Camping, at the dedicated climatic refugee camp sites. In tent with a grounding cable. Sometimes there were more nnlights than at home and it’s a mess to try to block it all on the spot. But overall good sleep and easy dawn wake up. Like at home I find myself unable to catch dawn when cloudy.
Unlike at home I seldom heard the faint tinnitus.
Mostly good moods, even by cloudy days

### Paleodocteur — 2017-11-23T21:06:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-228758>

A short journey back to northern H1c3 motherland. A day trip in a zapping train. Doing ok on the way up .

### Paleodocteur — 2017-11-23T21:08:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-228760>

Staying there would usually turns me back to default mode.
By that I mean the low drive coach-potato that I used to be. It didn’t happen too much when I went back in the last few years, because I tend to do better.
It even seems to have a beneficial effect...on me.

### Paleodocteur — 2017-11-23T21:21:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-228762>

My native field has become as polluted as anywhere else , the nearby church is beaming 0,500mW/m^2 at the bathtub window. Where I could see sun rise...
ELFs are « bouncing » to twenty microT . the cornet jumps , green,orange, green, orange even when central breaker is OFF, I wonder what that’s from.

### Paleodocteur — 2017-11-23T21:23:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-228763>

This old light fixture in the kitchen stinks.
Induction plates must have had their share in her cancer relapse too.
« Mum did you know a 2005 prospective study with EMF holters showed higher breast cancer rates in the higher tertile ? » - doesn’t work
« Stay away from the induction plates Mum » - doesn’t work
« Please block the street lights » - doesn’t work
« Go to sleep early please Mum » - does work a bit , she may have gained 2 hours over 5 years.

### Paleodocteur — 2017-11-23T21:25:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-228764>

In five years of advices , I could only make her go to bed a little earlier, but I suspect it’s because "you know they talked about it on the radio". So when we come to visit and all lights go off early , it is a very early curfew she as to endure. May be not so good either.

### Paleodocteur — 2017-11-23T21:26:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-228765>

I was unable to prevent her aortic stenosis, nor the open heart afternoon surgery. But she is recovering well.
Incidentally the local newspaper just released a local but large cohort study « Better to have your heart open in the afternoon : twice more likely to get complications when open in the morning . Doctors scratching their head»

### Paleodocteur — 2017-11-23T21:27:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-228766>

Now also when she walks in the park she glances at the sun.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-11-23T21:38:57+0200

Small progress I guess.

### Paleodocteur — 2017-11-23T21:43:09+0200

On the wall I see black and white photographs at the beach with my grandpa. She had lots of freckles. Her white hair used to be red so she tried to avoid sun burns very early.

### Paleodocteur — 2017-11-23T21:44:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-228771>

My home has much lower readings than hers. Yet my creativity there seems to have bounced back. I made more in three days than I did in three months. I am unable to see why.

### Paleodocteur — 2017-11-23T21:50:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-228773>

The TV stays off and we open those old wooden games that belonged to grandPa

### Paleodocteur — 2017-11-23T21:51:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-228774>

Unconditional Mother love and a bouncy MKG ?
Geotropic healer? Even if it is now polluted, and has a poor Yeld - we had filtered sunshine; It would make sense that my mitos and I get better on the land my maternal haplotype was doing well. GranMa was in good shape and some of her 6 sisters made it to their nineties.

### Paleodocteur — 2017-11-26T17:42:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-228888>

Or a good sign of relief ?

### Paleodocteur — 2017-11-26T17:48:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-228889>

The boost only lasted so long.
On the way south stormy weather.
Ruching to buy a meridian man.
In a bookstore, looking for Vital question .
"No sir we don't have it but you can check our science books over there..."
I wish I didn't
inadvertently stumbled on book , just glanced on some way old photographs and patents that I can’t seem to be able to erase from my failing memory.

### Paleodocteur — 2017-11-26T17:49:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-228890>

### Paleodocteur — 2017-11-26T18:03:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-228891>

Luxury shielded from ionospheric interferences (was it written 0.25 Farad?) Speedy metallic tube going south . Zap Zap Zhappy train . Faster Faster ….

### Paleodocteur — 2017-12-22T21:55:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-230867>

Dear Santa
This year I have been a good 50Hz boy
I took my cold garden shower all autumn and winter , every morning at dawn ( except when the hose gets frozen or I have time to go to the beach) .
From his window the neighbor can witness my steaming skin (hopefully he can not see that I am peeing blue, he would think I am crazy).
After I feed the guinea pigs I grab most sunrises .
It is tricky as they recently replaced the old sodium vapor street lights with crispy LEDs . Trying different scenarios.

### Paleodocteur — 2017-12-22T21:56:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-230868>

Although everyone around complains this autumn sucks and really started midJuly , I seem to have gone through it better than previous years.
I can go with one or two meals a day provided EMFs conditions are met. 6 weeks ago I ramped up nuts cheese an oysters….And dark C3 theobroma cocoa.
Cravings are getting rare.

### Paleodocteur — 2017-12-22T21:57:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-230869>

Moobs are gone, and I have been loosing upper belly fat fast. too fast. so I up the cheese. I have more muscle mass without exercice. It’s like 2011 all over again.

### Paleodocteur — 2017-12-22T21:58:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-230870>

But that wasn’t my goal. I have so many articles and so many great books (I know they are wrong but one got to start somewhere) in the Library to read .
And with candle light, it's slow.
Dear Santa , I want to gain a better functioning brain so I can make complete sense of all this. Sure lately we have connected many dots, and with Great Amazement! But it still seems like a beginning more than a conclusion ( another Zeno effect ).

### Paleodocteur — 2017-12-22T21:59:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-230871>

I don’t think I ever was a fast nor a good thinker, It’s just that my curious large head would sens things. So I don’t know if it is even possible to rewire that cortex. I guess I should just be thankful I escaped the escalating brain fogginess I use to be in. But I want more.

### Paleodocteur — 2017-12-22T22:23:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-230876>

P.S.
Dear Santa I also was a very patient boy, a good indigogoboy . I wish I would have received my Q-toy before Christmas, because yeld is lowering here too and I have trains to take and some leftover deutons to lift.…
But its’ okay, I know you’ve been busy. May be Christmass 2018 then?

Feliz Navidad ! Muchos Besos

Social carbing fallout. I may have stained the phone with oyster juice and lemon.

Now I want to see what to do when I « Perform » in the Titanic belly, or more likely right after it. Will chose wellness again tonight.

### Paleodocteur — 2018-02-03T18:13:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-234041>

More instabilities
Spring is already making brief attempts then clouds quickly come back.
Bright cold mornings alternate with gloomy days. Then again.
How that pattern alternates during nighttimes dictates the tone of the day.
Only 0.02uT on LF30.
Flowers are getting out.
But for humans these temptative rebirths followed by sudden withdrawal, when nothing is consolidated, is taxing.
No D yet.

### Paleodocteur — 2018-02-03T18:15:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-234042>

Feeling strong in the morning rise then collapsing around dusk. More instabilities when deviating from orthoLuxy. I should not. Amygdalas misfire. (Although they ought to)
Blasted on tuesday , blasted on friday .
went from intermittent fasting to intermittent chocolate splurging. Deuterium/Hydrogen yo-yoing. Cognitive dissonance.
Then the Revenant trailer didn’t help.
Worst night in a long time. Tried emergency sleep program in the middle of the night but had to turn the phone on to do so > mixed result. Good news is I may have released so much UV that I could at last fully flex the finger.
Forcing

### Paleodocteur — 2018-02-03T18:27:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-234044>

For a long time have been wondering what or who is leading my hand when fried in the Library:
I had to drop back Feynman’s cause this brain needs more summer light to function.
But I make progress, I know my multiplication tables now.
Instead found much more palatable Franck Ryan’s. Great book about LINE SINE and HERV and Syncitin !
Also found another fatty gem :Isotopic Geology by Claude Allegre . Our former provocative education minister and early climatosceptic. He was very controversial fifteen years ago. Then they attacked him, ridiculed him, vilified him, and nobody talks about him anymore. But that might change.
Just opened it, all about D/H and cloud isotopic oxygen fractionation .
Isotopic Paleothermometre…says we should be heading for a cooling.

Forcing…

this book makes me wonder what is the extent of our cosmogenic nature.
Earth receive a proton bombardment from the Cosmic Galactic Radiation (one GeV) .
They get moved or accelerated by surrounding EM fields.
They get stopped in the surface of matter , while neutrons travel a little deeper.

Protons interact with atmospheric atoms , give secondary neutrons whose energy decrease to the point of becoming “thermic”
( kT around 0.025 eV where did I hear about that energy again?) with is their sweet spot for transforming the nucleus.

### Paleodocteur — 2018-03-18T19:32:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-237435>

organic liquids
in water at 298K where permitivity=78.5(!?), rc~0.7 nm so that the probability of recombination is small. In contrast to this in a hydrocarbon such as cyclohexane, for which permittivity~2, ~30nm, most of the electrons and ions produced recombine. Yields of free ions in these hydrocarbons are 0.005 micromolsJ-1.
More polar molecules , i.e. ethanol show larger values of Gfi, thermalisation distance~//from Ggi. for non polar liquids Gfi increase with the sphericity of molecule
Primary radicals are hydrated electrons (e-aq) powerful reductant Eo=-2.87 V and hydroxyl radical, powerful oxidant Eo =1.90V and 2.72V in acidic solution
H. is not important in neutral solution, but becomes major reductant E=-2.31V in acidic solution(!!!) (e-aq) +H+ ——>H.
The hydrated electron may be visualised as an excess electron surrounded by oriented water molecules (Pix),
precise details not yet settled.
It reacts by transferring into a vacant orbital of an acceptor molecule
< e-aq + S^n ——> S^n-1 >
Rate constant from~16 l mol-1s-1 for H2O to the ‘diffusion-controlled limit’ (??) although the measured energy is invariably small ( 6-30kJ mol-1) with suggests that the entropy of activation is the dominant kinetic parameter (Q: biological relevant?) . This can be rationalised in terms of the accessibility of a vacant orbital on S for the electron to enter and explains why solvated electrons are sufficiently long lived to be observed by pulse radiolysis in liquid such as water, simple alcohols, ethers , amines and alkanes whose molecules have low-energy vacant orbitals. Organic molecules with low-energy vacant orbitals(e.g. most aromatics, halides, thiols, dissulfides, carbonyl-and nitro-compounds)(!) all react rapidly, e-aq acting as a nucleophile. Thus it is greatly enhanced by electron-withdrawing substituents adjacent to double bounds or attached to aromatic rings (!!!).
Although the first step is electron addition to a vacant orbital, in some cases, notably with organic halides (RX), bond breakage occurs very rapidly….
“ e-aq+ RX——> (RX.-)——> R. + X- “ ( //F?//)
Inorganic ions react rapidly with e-aq when their reduction potentials are more positive than -2.9V ., cation react faster than anions, reflecting the negative charge of e-aq.
“.OH + Sn —> Sn+1 + OH-“ rapid
…..
“.OH + Cl- <——> HOCl.-“
“HOCl- + H+ ——> Cl. + H2O”
…..
Although it is a reducing radical, the reactions of the hydrogen atom with organic molecules are similar to those of .OH. Thus it adds to C=C and aromatic rings and abstract H from C-H bonds in saturated compounds (?!!!=Lyases?);
In the latter case it is generally les reactive and more selective than .OH. In its reactions with metals ions H. is less reactive than e-aq and in some cases it reacts via the formation of a hydride complex and effectively becomes an oxidant:
“ \[H+\]
H. + Fe2+ —> \[Fe3+,H-\] —> Fe3+ + H2 “ (?—> beware)

Generation of secondary radicals
Radiolysis of W produces approximately equal yells of reducing (e-aq and H+) oxidising (OH.) radicals but in application they try to separate those by
a) interconversion of the primary radical into a single kind
b) converting all the primary radicals into a single kind of secondary radical
c) by removing the unwanted primary radical by reaction to form a relatively inert secondary radical…(Yawn)…. … the advantage of organic liquids over water is the range of solvent polarity (!) available so that species that are insoluble in water can be studied in a suitable solvent or mixture of solvents.
… Alcohols …. CH3OH used for one-electron reduction of quinones (??)
… e-s + CCl4 ——> CCl3. + Cl-.. .. investigation of one-electron of metallotetraphenylporphyrins ….

### Paleodocteur — 2018-03-18T19:34:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-237437>

Hydrocarbons
In theses non polar liquids, geminate ion recombination is the dominant process…> formation of electronically excited states of the solvent molecule that are liable to have quite high energies. Unless fragmentation of the molecule take place through bond breakage, the excited molecule fall to their lowest excited state through internal conversion within ~10^-12s. After this…~ photochemistry: ISC, ET, Luminescence, bond rupture etc… (!!!!)
Aromatic compounds are much more resistant to radiation damage than alkanes and alkenes, because the excitation energy is shared by ALL(?) the Pi-electrons….

Chapter 3 THE SOLVATED ELECTRON: A SINGULAR CHEMICAL SPECIES
IN 1808 SIR H DAVID(sic) REPORTED THE INTENSE BLUe COLOURING OBTAINED BY DISSOLVING ALKALI METALS IN AMMONIA…
….4 ways of producing it
…some physical properties of the solvated electrons: chemistry , electrochemistry, radiolysis, photolysis
volume cavity radius in water 2.5A (derived from volume expansion)
mobility: 100 000 times faster than ions
in water 1.9X10^3 cm^2 V^-1 S^-1
Optical absorption : varies with the solvent. in pure water intense broad structureless band with a maximum around 720 nm. max of absorption band shifts to higher wavelength as the dielectric constant of the solvent diminishes. but mostly governed by molecular structure of the liquid. Q simulation indicated that the electron ground state is 1S-like localised state and the excited states are three non degenerate 2p-like states, also bound and localised, follow by a band of delocalised states. Hence , the broad absorption band of the electron correspond mostly to an 1S —> 2p transition with a contribution the transition from the bound state to the continuum at high energies
….
Nordlund probing the electron at attosecound

### Paleodocteur — 2018-03-18T19:40:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-237441>

Chapter 4 “radiolysis of water: a puzzling case”
….it has been known for a long time that, when carefully degassed and irradiated with low LET radiation such as X-rays or 60Co gamma rays , pure water gives rise to almost no products, the amount of which remains at a very low state concentration. It seems that water decomposes at very beginning of the irradiation and then becomes inert. But for high LET radiation such as alpha rays, water decomposition occurs with the formation of molecular hydrogen, hydrogen peroxyde and molecular oxygen. When the water contains air other solutes, water decomposition is completely suppressed at low LET but still occurs under alpha rays irradiation.
The explanation for this behaviour, which has paramount importance for N…industry, was given by Allen. After WW II , Allen depicted … that considers the production and consumption of H2. The key role of OH. , H2 and O2 involved in the chain as a carrier or a breaker is clear…… …. …. The molecular products are formed in the nanoseconds range and their recombination takes place in the millisecond range.
….Allen:
First note that the global water radiolysis reaction can be written:
“ H2O -^^^^-> H. , OH. , e-aq ,H2, H2O2 +HO.2 + H3O+ “
We observe that O2 is not the primary species in the water radiolysis; it is formed by secondary reactions.The key reaction which explains the unique role of H2 is :
“ OH. + H2 —> H2O + H. “ (1)
this reaction converts an oxidising radical hydroxyl OH. into a powerful reducing species, the H. atom. The first step is the reduction of O2 in H2O2
“ H. + O2 —> HO.2 “ (2)
“ HO.2 + Ho.2 —> H2O2 + O2 “ (3)
the second step is a chain reaction between H. and H2O2
“ H. + H2O2 ——> OH. +H2O “ (4)
followed by reaction 1 with gives back an H. atom.
Whilst O2 is still present, the chain reaction does not take place, because the rate constant of reaction 2, which is a chain breaker, is 200 times larger than the rate constant of reaction 4 WICH is A CHAIN CARRIER.

### Paleodocteur — 2018-03-18T19:41:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-237442>

There is another chain termination which is important to explain to explain the paradoxical behaviour of water radiolysis.
“ OH. + H2O2 —> H. + HO.2 “ (5)
We can see that H2O2 is both a chain carrier ( reaction 4) (//<Bach>//) and a chain breaker ( reaction5). When its concentration increases too much it prevents the chain reaction by forming O2 (reaction5 followed by reaction 3).
The rate constant k5 and k1 have the same order of magnitude, so it is necessary to add sufficient concentration of H2 TO INSURE THAT REACTION 1is always FASTER than reaction 5.
The net results of these reactions is
“ O2 + H2 + 2H. —> 2H2O “ (6)
Reaction 6 shows that we need at least two h. radicals for one H2 and O2.
At low LET radiation like gamma and X-rays, this easily achieved and the water behaves as if it was completely inert under irradiation. At high LET (alpha rays) the ratio of the molecular products over the free radicals increases and there are comparatively too few H. to O2 atoms ., the recombination of H2 and O2 is thus incomplete, the chain reaction between H2 and H2O2 cannot start and water decomposition is observers.
This set of reactions also clearly explains why water radiolysis takes place when some solutes are present. Most of the chemical species react with the H. atom or the OH. radical and prevent the chain reaction.

Meissner effect 1933 Berlin. Expulsion of Magnetic lines. Screening current.
Cornelius Jacobus Gorter 1934+ Casimir:2 currents
The London brothers :quantum chemistry
1933 Oxford+ICI —>Dusting off Ohm’s law.
Sommerfeld, (Fröhlich, Heisenberg, Heitler, London, Pauli ) Fermi Dirac statistics over dude’s model…incomplete>>London’s equations>>>electrons acts together, singing in unisson. current linked not to applied tension but to a Magnetic potential Vector ( from Maxwell). Current exist by itself, enveloped in its own Magnetic field.
Exterior fields were excluded , but were slightly penetrating inside the superconductor on a short distance.tied to mass, charge and number of charge carrier. Depth measured.
What it meant : breaking certainties
Shrodinger amplitude and phase

Max Born 1926: probability depend on amplitude not on the phase of the wave. Wave function module |psy|^2. Sommerfeld had ignored the phase. but realised he shouldn’t for they all had the same value—>very stable wave fonction: RIGIDITY> no diffraction. stationary state > London >SC~giant atom> exhibit quantum property at macroscopic scale. 1948: “Magflux is quantised”…but tiny. 1950 Fritz London book+++

John Bardeen, (invent a point transistor) and Walter Brattain in Bell lab ( under William Shockley (irritable and secretive, developper of jonction transistor> lab in Montain view> father of Silicon Valley and all electronic devices. Hubris> failed))
1950 phone call to Barden about isotopic effect. republish his article. pb . they didn’t account for Coulomb repulsion and they were to strong compared to web vibrations.
But if you add screen effect then it’s OK.

Brian Pippard (radars) Surface resistance. new scale of penetration : coherence length. gradual transition. One point responds to local and environmental field in the coherence length.
Mid 50’s Bardeen hires Leon Cooper and robert Schrieffer. focused on one pair first. A small attractive interaction was enough for “instable system tending to pairing”. Electron-phonon coupling higher in poor conductors. Non existing in good conductors (Au, Ag, Cu)
Gap. 1956 Glover and Pinkham experiment!!! Shine IR on SC separate pairs and E is absorbed….
BCS theory A pair of dancers separated in each corner of the room by millions of other dancers yet still synchronised.
A superconductor must have a specified PHASE. A BCS wave fonction is coherent state. BCS 1957 master piece. 2nd Nobel in 1973.

### Paleodocteur — 2018-05-30T22:59:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-242400>

Nowadays I try not to bring articles nor blogs nor meter when I am on a trip. I am trying to give myself and them a break and stop thinking about it all the time. be more present.

This time I brought the cornet and CPC21.

My sensitivity has evolved within last five years. It varies with season and time of day. I feel them more in winter and equinoxes, more towards the end of day or at the very beginning of it. More indoor. My fears of them have gone down. Usualy I just take notice that I can sense a signal on my skin at a particular place and a particular moment. My “threshold” for skin perception seems to be around 0.002mW/m^2

### Paleodocteur — 2018-05-30T23:08:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-242403>

For the grid I seem much less sensitive than a couple years ago. Turning the fuses on and off everyday used to be a pain. not anymore . I could be bothered by sensing the E field of light and power strips around 20V/m at about a meter away, not anymore. I sense when my BV is above normal. Threshold around 0.5v. I sense the transitioning more than the steady state. I grew up in the grid.
I feel better outside or interacting with people. I stopped working in the morning. I study in the garden. That makes my afternoons more dynamic. Even then I know I still need to take outside breaks. A gulp at the sky. What animal would feel good stuck on a chair 5 hours in a row?

I don’t feel my best inside the house, even though the readings are low 0.008mW, so I tend to stay outside or in spots away from wires and keeping eye contact with sky. Bedroom readings are low but we experience a tiny tinitus as soon as we lay down. It usualy vanishes during the night. I spontaneously wake up at dawn and when clouds permit we walk to the street with proper orientation that will allow us to stargaze.

So in this camping , within the pine trees I felt great. I avoided the swimming pool where people and artificial waves gather.
But even outside in this heaven, or because of it I could still feel a signal on top of the dune where we had breakfast. from a tower I could not even see: 0.02mW! I had moment of discouragement and thought with this reading we might as well go home. But It vanished as the day went on. provided I spent enough time outside I usualy would not feel anything around noon. Here neither, even though many people on the beach were using their bidirectional device. But, as usualy, later that day when we were waiting for sunset I could sense the buzzing of a group 10 meters away. Cornet reading was low but pulsed.ii|ii|ii|ii|

Biking home , botteling the feeling. of long day.

Why does my feeling vary?
Does it matter? Meaning the fact that I sense it sometimes not at other times. Is it more harmful at those moment ? Is it a good sign or a bad sign when I don’t feel it? My redox varies with season and day? Because of my stress response? Better sensing = worse response? Poor sensing = better redox = no stress response or stronger HPA axis? Stronger testosterone response? Poor sensing = poor senses or poor awareness ? Certainly focusing on something else or someone else helps a lot. But is it mere distraction? When eye or brain or skin are baked or constantly baking what is left of senses? Slight nose clogging is when I usually get alerted my BV is rising . May be everyone senses it and only a few take notice and only a handful of them get alarmed and even fewer will do something about it.
Kind of when a house is starting to burn.

-   Do you smell this…?
-   \- What?
-   \- I don’t know , like… something is burning.
-   \- …

They recently did testing on microwaved rats. for some reason the animals were avoiding the rooms they had previously been irradiated. I see this kind of behaviour in me and other humans so often. But we have bigger brains. They feel something is wrong but their thinking enable them to find so many reasons to stay in their discomfort.

### Paleodocteur — 2018-05-30T23:12:41+0300

I was thinking of self-assessment with MMS but just assumed it’s not sensitive enough yet. I need a more refined test. So that’s what I was thinking when I saw the iris : “go, don’t let another year pass, go , drop the readings, get out and stay in the sun and paint it before it wilters, it’s going to be a long beautiful day.”

Last year I found in a botany book that it only takes a few milliseconds to alter tubulin network in a leave…

### Paleodocteur — 2018-07-01T18:00:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-244676>

I thought 5G would spare us another year or two but I noticed that our telcos have been using unidirectional emitters in the tens twenties thirties and sixty GHz. I believe it is used to broadcast TV, or TV on cell phones. I was more focused on 4G deployment in the area so I hadn’t notice these higher frequencies in the tens of Giga until the recent talks about 5G.
Some have been around for years. I can’t mesure them with the meter but we have mandatory declaration with dates of modification. And there have been some in 2016 and a lot in spring 2018 Now I understand why I tend to avoid some areas, why this winter was bad and why more people are going down.

### Paleodocteur — 2018-07-11T12:20:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-245607>

Summer solstice results after a winter at the 46th lat, spring outside 5 mornings a week
unsupplemented D 48 ng
urea went down from 0.32 this winter to 0.19 (min)
S-DHEA up a bit 400ug/dl
down sides:
Testo goes down to 2.9 ng from 4.6 a year ago. //Good parallel with weight gains spring moobs and low libido. I have to cover the junk later in the morning.
CRP rising at 8mg while ESR stays low at 7. probably related to the allergy outburst. And something else.

Going to test higher altitude

### Paleodocteur — 2018-07-29T11:54:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-246883>

Walks in a nature preserve . a day long trip away.
Learning altitude. Not to high, 1500-2000m. lower people density.
Crossing quite a few. “Why bare foot? Are you a penitent?”
Old people walking fast, passing us.
Untrained , to heavy, loaded with water and food and other unnecessary stuff.

altitude and effort seems to make humans more humain
. Horrendous lightings though.

### Paleodocteur — 2018-07-29T12:47:04+0300

funny enough a long time ago ,prisoners were held in the “Clock tower”.

### Paleodocteur — 2018-07-29T13:27:59+0300

Panurge blocking our way. Meeting the original sheep

and the bitter shepard.
This year winter has been early gloomy and awfully long. Had to feed the herd more cereals. Held is low. Those two lambs should have not been born in july. Is milk deuterium content altered? What about the cheese?

### Paleodocteur — 2018-07-29T13:31:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-246912>

These last summers
montain tips are devoid of snow.
Could a cell phone in the back-pocket of a shepard melt it all down?

Funny how colours can tell geologic times.

### Paleodocteur — 2018-07-29T13:39:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-246914>

I
Most villages are in the valley. Bad choice. Small population density yes but with spikes in summer/winter. Cellmasts are at gunpoint. and damned dams, power stations uses Gravity to produce AC.
In the north-south valley there is no real sunset and no real dawn. And we really felt a difference when we started to miss their callings.

Overall, altitude felt good (There must ways to make it even better) but not the greatest option.

### Paleodocteur — 2018-09-15T20:43:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-251062>

Back to the low lands . The marches
This summer was bright. And still is. Will draw D. One doesn’t bath twice in the same flow. Although days are rapidly shortening. I can still sense the radiation pressure when laying down.

### Paleodocteur — 2018-09-15T20:46:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-251063>

### Paleodocteur — 2018-09-15T20:48:28+0300

### Paleodocteur — 2018-09-15T20:56:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-251065>

Meanwhile some are losing it on the Upper West Side

and in Merignac where 5G testings are happening.

It is not that 5G gets you to suffer from new disease yet.
Depression is an old one . But it just seems that rhythmic patterns are changing.

With more acute automnal phases and slower recovery.

### Paleodocteur — 2018-09-15T20:57:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-251066>

[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/thumb_img_4557_1024-jpg.8878/)

### Paleodocteur — 2018-09-15T21:00:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-251067>

### Paleodocteur — 2018-09-15T21:01:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-251068>

### Paleodocteur — 2018-09-15T21:17:43+0300

do we have anything around 60Hz?

### drezy — 2018-09-23T00:26:38+0300

How did you know that I do that from my roof as my favorite hobby? It's quite a good likeness of me. I must say the "pang" vs 'bang" seems more accurately descriptive. Good job French language. Its like "Pow" and "Bang" worked closely with each other for many years and then had an aventure extraconjugale. From that the enfant naturel "Pang" was obviously born.

Your questions remind me of QT#6. Am I confusing the topics?

I have a hunch, bonhomme de livres, that you and I could end up exponentiating our reading lists. I've already told my wife that I'll likely be found dead someday under a pile of collapsed books. I'm at peace with that end.

From that French page:
"The intrinsic quantum determination of things is a blur, like the blur of Boltzmann, which, contrary to what classical physics seemed to suggest - unpredictability in the product would persist even if we hold everything measurable"

Maybe not quite the same but on a larger time scale is Boltzmann's fluctuations mentioned here @ 56:00 - 59:00

Also at around 1:04 "freak" probabilities are mentioned.

I think there is still some "3 blind men and an elephant" going on there, but some truth, within context, is in that.

Full disclosure: That video is part of me trying to deprogram myself from thinking linearly that the universe "must" have progressed from a lower entropy state until now and the progression will continue. I'm in the process of unlearning that. I think it may have implications at small scales too.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-09-23T19:23:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-251588>

Calling the Cambrian explosion a freak event, however, has some merit.......on many levels. It offends Darwinism because the arrow of time argument is unsupported......but as a Black Swan event, it shows you nature is not about cause and effect but really about statistical probability. For me it is one of the greatest mysteries of biology.

### Paleodocteur — 2018-12-29T17:57:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-259179>

Sun cravings brought back a memory
The biggest IR panel I have ever seen
No electricity ( At least back in the 90s')

Men with brocken bones and non-union would travel from far far away to this outdoor clinic

The Medicine man would give them plants. Remove the POP and use little pieces of bark to cast their limbs .
In the crevices of the red cliff was a faint stream. A toguna ( pile of layers of shrug) would provide some moist and shadow
Their was nothing else to do but wait and talk while the sun was pounding the cliff.

### Paleodocteur — 2019-05-18T21:59:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-270779>

**EARTH'S ATMOSPHERE STRETCHES OUT TO THE MOON -- AND BEYOND**

[](https://sohowww.nascom.nasa.gov/hotshot/index.html/EarthAtmosphereMoonSOHOv5_web.jpg)
[Large](https://sohowww.nascom.nasa.gov/hotshot/index.html/EarthAtmosphereMoonSOHOv5.jpg) \[JPG, 1MB\], [Large](https://sohowww.nascom.nasa.gov/hotshot/index.html/EarthAtmosphereMoonSOHOv5.tiff) \[TIFF, 80MB\]

The outermost part of our planet's atmosphere extends well beyond the lunar orbit -- almost twice the distance to the Moon.

A recent discovery based on observations by the ESA/NASA Solar and Heliospheric Observatory, SOHO, shows that the gaseous layer that wraps around Earth reaches up to 630,000 km away, or 50 times the diameter of our planet. "_The Moon flies through Earth's atmosphere,_" says Igor Baliukin of Russia's Space Research Institute, lead author of the paper presenting the results. "We were not aware of it until we dusted off observations made over two decades ago by the SOHO spacecraft."

Where our atmosphere merges into outer space, there is a cloud of hydrogen atoms called the geocorona. One of the spacecraft instruments, SWAN, used its sensitive sensors to trace the hydrogen signature and precisely detect how far the very outskirts of the geocorona are. These observations could be done only at certain times of the year, when the Earth and its geocorona came into view for SWAN.

For planets with hydrogen in their exospheres, water vapour is often seen closer to their surface. That is the case for Earth, Mars and Venus. "_This is especially interesting when looking for planets with potential reservoirs of water beyond our Solar System,_" explains Jean-Loup Bertaux, co-author and former principal investigator of SWAN. The first telescope on the Moon, placed by Apollo 16 astronauts in 1972, captured an evocative image of the geocorona surrounding Earth and glowing brightly in ultraviolet light. "_At that time, the astronauts on the lunar surface did not know that they were actually embedded in the outskirts of the geocorona,_" says Jean-Loup.

**Cloud of hydrogen**
The Sun interacts with hydrogen atoms through a particular wavelength of ultraviolet light called Lyman-alpha, which the atoms can both absorb and emit. Since this type of light is absorbed by Earth's atmosphere, it can only be observed from space. Thanks to its hydrogen absorption cell, the SWAN instrument could selectively measure the Lyman-alpha light from the geocorona and discard hydrogen atoms further out in interplanetary space. The new study revealed that sunlight compresses hydrogen atoms in the geocorona on Earth's dayside, and also produces a region of enhanced density on the night side. The denser dayside region of hydrogen is still rather sparse, with just 70 atoms per cubic centimeter at 60,000 kilometers above Earth's surface, and about 0.2 atoms at the Moon's distance.

"_On Earth we would call it vacuum, so this extra source of hydrogen is not significant enough to facilitate space exploration,_" says Igor. The good news is that these particles do not pose any threat for space travelers on future crewed missions orbiting the Moon. "_There is also ultraviolet radiation associated to the geocorona, as the hydrogen atoms scatter sunlight in all directions, but the impact on astronauts in lunar orbit would be negligible compared to the main source of radiation -- the Sun,_" says Jean-Loup Bertaux.

[](https://sohowww.nascom.nasa.gov/hotshot/index.html/SOHO_observation_of_the_geocorona_web.jpg)
[Large](https://sohowww.nascom.nasa.gov/hotshot/index.html/SOHO_observation_of_the_geocorona_large.jpg) \[JPG, 1MB\], [Large](https://sohowww.nascom.nasa.gov/hotshot/index.html/SOHO_observation_of_the_geocorona.tiff) \[TIFF, 6.5MB\]

On the down side, the Earth's geocorona could interfere with future astronomical observations performed in the vicinity of the Moon. "_Space telescopes observing the sky in ultraviolet wavelengths to study the chemical composition of stars and galaxies would need to take this into account,_" adds Jean-Loup.

**The power of archives**
Launched in December 1995, the SOHO space observatory has been studying the Sun, from its deep core to the outer corona and the solar wind, for over two decades. The satellite orbits around the first Lagrange point (L1), some 1.5 million kilometres from Earth towards the Sun. This location is a good vantage point to observe the geocorona from outside.

SOHO's [SWAN instrument](http://swan.projet.latmos.ipsl.fr/) imaged Earth and its extended atmosphere on three occasions between 1996 and 1998. Jean-Loup and Igor's research team in Russia decided to retrieve this data set from the archives for further analysis. These unique views of the whole geocorona as seen from SOHO are now shedding new light on Earth's atmosphere.

"_Data archived many years ago can often be exploited for new science,_" says Bernhard Fleck, ESA SOHO project scientist. "_This discovery highlights the value of data collected over 20 years ago and the exceptional performance of SOHO._"

**More information**

-   Baliukin, I., J.-L. Bertaux, E. Quemerais, V. Izmodenov, and W. Schmidt. (2019), ["SWAN/SOHO Lyman-alpha mapping: the Hydrogen Geocorona extends well beyond the Moon"](https://agupubs.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/abs/10.1029/2018JA026136), J. Geophys. Res. Space Physics, 124. [https://doi.org/10.1029/2018JA026136](https://doi.org/10.1029/2018JA026136)

### Paleodocteur — 2019-10-20T20:21:22+0300

Last year I went on stage and called for a nightlight curfew in my town

100 000 more souls get six hours of straight night

Now the new fight is going to keep the street lights off in the morning so we don’t get blasted on our walk at dawn

oysters are a staple , people walk.

### Paleodocteur — 2023-02-26T13:04:11+0200

even if the place is very "developed".

despite the great swims.

### Paleodocteur — 2023-02-26T13:08:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-319763>

as a hypogonadic human-rat raised on floor seven of universe25 I never really dreamt of being successful.

A lot of amazement at Nature and my fellow humans.

I was curious of everything and your writings sent me on a quest for deeper Truth.

It started more than a decade ago and has never stoped.

as Zenon (Or Sherazade...) each step closer but never there yet.

### Paleodocteur — 2026-04-11T17:34:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-370706>

This winter was different since we decided not to go south. I was worried but it went well.
untill February. I was trying to use the cold as much as I could . Was doing well.Taking cold swims in the ocean on a regular basis. Temperature goes down to 8°Celsius.I felt great. night were long and I was keeping a tan. By December my vitamin D was 30 ng .

I didn't feel it when it happened but after one of those exhilarating bath in the rough waves I felt a dull pain in left Achilles.
I had to work and drive
Also in February EM-sensitivity was slowly rising.

### Paleodocteur — 2026-04-11T17:38:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-370707>

I decided that I had to go tropical anyway or things wouldn't get better.

### Paleodocteur — 2026-04-11T17:46:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-370708>

I had been hoping to go to Bourbon island for many years because I knew volcano was quite active. but it is a long flight and I was worried due to population density being one of the highest.. military bases and half population obese and 3 times our national prevalence for diabetes,
Interesting but not very promising.. health wise.
I was lucky enough to secure two weeks work there.

### Paleodocteur — 2026-04-11T17:57:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-370710>

12 h flight was indeed awful
But once outside I felt the benefit quite fast
Mood uplift on arrival.
UV index 12 + the poor sleep induced cortisol

E-sensitvity got exacerbated for the first week before it decreased.

Tinnitus decreased even inaudible in some areas of the caldera.

Tendonitis got better but took longer cause I had to drive around to scout for the right stream to bathe in

### Paleodocteur — 2026-04-11T18:09:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-370712>

Even though leptospirosis is no joke. I took any opportunity to ground and bathe. Most water is from tropical clouds that get stuck atop the montain and burst. They pretend to sell 'volcanic'water but it tasted 'heavy ' ( not a valid test)

### Paleodocteur — 2026-04-11T18:16:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-370713>

Long time ago they noticed the health benefits.
This thermal station had been abandoned but there was a small stream to enjoy.

### Paleodocteur — 2026-04-11T18:24:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-370714>

Despite their load of problems and diseases people were nice and smiley. Mostly the young adults are getting their hope squized by high unemployment, public housing density/modern lifestyle old religious clothing and modern jobs/ devices addiction. Old folks looked solid.

### Paleodocteur — 2026-04-11T19:03:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-370716>

i saw more diabetics in two week practice than in ten years in metropole. When asking my colleagues why ? "Oh it's genetics"
I don't know Dravidian haplotype but it's unlikely to be same as African/ mahories. Both have same rate of exploding young diabetes. Both have very dark skins ( unless they get old inside..).
In two generations they went from walking barefoot in the field to scrolling on 5G while driving one hour to the fifth floor of a air conditioned building.
Autoimmunity once much less prevalent than métropole is on the rise. T1D.

Blame it on sugarcane? Rhum? exhausted slaves in the field? Domestic Abuses?Coca-Cola?

Or glyphosate runoffs? French TV? ALAN . Government housing indoor living+ tech . ?
Some places on the coastline had horrible measurement way above 8V/m .radar?

Was the Omega tower our Sanguine type antenna?

Did it whip out the coral too?
Or is it only sunscreen?
I haden't noticed coral fluoresces in the morning.

### Paleodocteur — 2026-04-11T19:10:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-370717>

I got lucky it recently erupted. Got to see the lava flowing down to the ocean. Local people worship their volcano. everyone was so excited to go visit it. Is it only for the thrill of the show or do they instinctively know how much our lives depend on it?

### Paleodocteur — 2026-04-11T19:16:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-370718>

It very felt good to guide a few patients towards sunlight.
Remarkably a young farmer picked it up right away. Analogy to their chicken sensitivity to light.

Some people just already know what to do

### Paleodocteur — 2026-04-11T19:20:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-370720>

I usually don't need reading glasses but my eyesight got very variable. Especially at higher altitude (1000m like in the Pyrenees last summer). Subacute presbyopia seem to depend on contrast between previous outdoor exposure and surrounding emfs. Altitude dehydration?

### Paleodocteur — 2026-04-11T19:25:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-370721>

There were also some beautiful encounters with older folks full of vitality.
There are bells in the village
An old painter from the caldera who never needed reading glasses

### Paleodocteur — 2026-04-11T19:31:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-370722>

Despite work I built a tan and I was told my eyes got darker
Flying back was horrendous but spring is here and it's gorgeous.
I kept my great mood. Presbyopia still fluctuate. Hunger came back.
I kept most of my tan . Vit D disappointing 30ng. EM-sensitivity low.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-04-11T23:21:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impressions-soleil-levant.12935/post-370730>

>

> I don't know Dravidian haplotype but it's unlikely to be same as African/ mahories. Both have same rate of exploding young diabetes. Both have very dark skins ( unless they get old inside..).

# Thread: Tongue and lip tie in babies?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tongue-and-lip-tie-in-babies.12936/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hope — 2015-02-15T22:45:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tongue-and-lip-tie-in-babies.12936/post-155711>

So I have a dear friend who had an adorable baby boy a year ago, on a WAPF diet, with some seafood. Generally very healthy, but has tongue and lip tie.

Anyone here know of causes for this? She is going to have a dentist remove them, she said, but I wonder if there are other things to be done.......

She did have MTHFR issues and took the methylated folate.

TIA.

### Jude — 2015-02-16T00:07:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tongue-and-lip-tie-in-babies.12936/post-155717>

> [Hope said:](/goto/post?id=155711)
>
> So I have a dear friend who had an adorable baby boy a year ago, on a WAPF diet, with some seafood. Generally very healthy, but has tongue and lip tie.
>
> Anyone here know of causes for this? She is going to have a dentist remove them, she said, but I wonder if there are other things to be done.......
>
> She did have MTHFR issues and took the methylated folate.
>
> TIA.

Interesting question Hope......do hope JK answers.....know a family where grandma, one daughter, and one grandchild have had it. Interestingly, they all claim to be 'bleeders', which kinda smacks of MTHFR but, that's my take as they don't know about MTHFR.
Have recently met a young mum with a 1298 who has had a bub with tongue tie which was clipped soon after birth. Breast feeding was very difficult prior to being clipped a couple of days after birth.
Never heard that tongue tie could be associated until MTHFR until it was mentioned in an email the other day by Dr Ben Lynch.

### nicld — 2015-02-16T00:52:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tongue-and-lip-tie-in-babies.12936/post-155723>

My first daughter had a pretty good lip tie and we were going to get it cut till she fell and broke it herself. I think one of my girls had their tongue tie cut buty for the life of me i cannot remember which one.

### Hope — 2015-02-16T02:59:39+0200

Thanks for sharing! My friend knew about the MTHFR...and I read it can be genetic so your example makes sense.

I just really wonder about this, as I'd never heard about it before.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-02-16T06:21:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tongue-and-lip-tie-in-babies.12936/post-155749>

I believe it is the most caudal of the oral manifestations of cleft lip and palate and is usually tied to altered migration and altered apoptosis and autophagy and always ties back to mom and dads altered circadian biology and this is why there is a familial disposition........people learn and do as their clan te
aches right or wrong

### cantweight — 2015-02-16T06:31:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tongue-and-lip-tie-in-babies.12936/post-155750>

After the procedure my youngest kept saying "my front teeth feel huge!"

### Erik — 2015-02-17T16:07:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.12961/post-155898>

Im 19, male, living in Argentina.

Current symptoms: Rosacea- mild digestive issues frequently- kinda electric ittching after a lot of EMF exposure- not very good sleep because of VERY frequent urination- allergic to practically every fruit or vegetable, so i only eat raw beef or organ meat and raw honey.

Last symptoms. -A month ago started havin Body odor after a week eating pasteurized youghurt and a lot of non-local raw honey. The odor VASTLY improved since i started megadosing probiotics.
\- A week ago, after sleeping on my mothars office ( with a tremendous sinthetic- like smell, i dont know what product was), i slept REALLY BAD, had a lot of nightmares, and from that day till today, i had brain fog 3 hours like every day. Coul it be that i intoxicated with that smell?? or is it the new pollen i took for trhee days?? or it has to do with the body odor?? (candida, H. Pylori maybe???) IF the blood brain barrier is leaking beacuse of EMF, an infection like those could go into the brain??????

PD:My rosacea goes away with im in a zero EMF enviromnent, also my fruit-casued acne goes away. Would my brain fog also leave in a EMF free zone??

Should i try colloidal silver??? (if it is an infection)

Could the brian fog be caused by the lactose excipient on my probiotic pills?

### yewwei.tan — 2015-02-19T01:40:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.12961/post-156000>

Personally, I see too many varied complications and reactions when using fermented foods to cure a damaged gut . Some people do great, others suffer even more. Right now, I see no useful information to be gained even with all the gut testing that we can do.

I'd rather have people eat Animal Fibre instead ... which provides the body with butyrate in the distal colon and lets the GI tract sort out the gut bacteria situation via it's own feedback mechanisms.

My recommendations to Matt apply here as well: [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.ph...ould-use-some-advice.12816/page-3#post-155295](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/15-year-old-who-could-use-some-advice.12816/page-3#post-155295)

From that post:

Personally, my recommendation is to just get some tendons and seafood shells into your system. Start slow here.

Recommended food sources:

-   Cuts of beef that are full of tendons
-   Prawn shells

Prawn shells are easy enough, just pan fry the prawns and eat the whole thing . I'd say 3-4 whole prawns (about 100+g / 4oz) produces a therapeutic effect.

For the beef, just buy a cut that would traditionally be used for slow-cooking or stewing, and then pan fry it like you would any other steak, cut it into decently large chunks, then chew minimally before swallowing. Cuts like Osso Bucco, Beef shins (usually called gravy beef), and oyster blade work well and taste decent. About 250g / 8oz of such meat is where I see a therapeutic effect.

Some people will get nausea a couple hours after trying this. In general, that is a sign that your gut flora is adapting. I would view it as a positive clinical sign, unless the nausea is either really bad, or sustained for >4 hours.​

More links regarding Animal Fibre

\- [http://caloriesproper.com/animal-fibre](http://caloriesproper.com/animal-fibre)
\- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.ph...ntial-through-hydride-ions.10343/#post-118768](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/do-raw-foods-improve-redox-potential-through-hydride-ions.10343/#post-118768)
\- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.ph...tochondrial-redox-compound.12674/#post-153724](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/molecular-hydrogen-h2-ultimate-mitochondrial-redox-compound.12674/#post-153724)

....

### Jack Kruse — 2015-09-22T23:36:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.12961/post-177619>

Rosacea: A lack of UV light.

Increased expression of cathelicidin antimicrobial peptide (CAMP) is related to the pathogenesis of rosacea. CAMP plays a crucial role in antimicrobial defenses, such as the killing of mycobacteria. CAMP gene expression is regulated by vitamin D-dependent (VDR) and vitamin D-independent (C/EBPα) transcription factors. VDR-dependent CAMP expression is sufficient during the summer months in Nordic countries, but insufficient during Nordic winters, due to low ultraviolet (UV) levels. This can also be due to light stressed environments in strong UV places as well. Historically, the Celts may have overcome this geographical disadvantage of deficient CAMP production during the winter through an as-yet undefined acquired mutation that activates the alternative vitamin D-independent CAMP promoter C/EBPα. C/EBPα is the downstream transcription factor of Toll-like receptor (TLR)-mediated innate immune reactions and endoplasmic reticulum (ER) stress responses. At the molecular level, all clinical trigger factors for rosacea can be regarded as ER stressors. UV light can fix that.

The bio hack: A reptile light to prove you live in a poor quantum yield region for UV light. The mechanism of Finsen’s UV treatment of lupus vulgaris by UVA/B is critical- and ER stress-mediated upregulation of CAMP expression happens from this light alone. Rosacea could therefore be described as an epigenetic modification in people born into a bad light quantum yield world like the Celts’ were. In essence biology's epigenome gave you “inborn Finsen lamp” in your skin..........if this persists then it tells you that your light environment is toxic to your quantum yield.

I started going to campus on bike, and half an hour earlier stay in the sun reading a little bit and looking almost at the sun (not directly). My face is red cause i burnt myself but my rosacea (irritation, pustules) is 95% gone. My brain fog is gone. My anxiety for yoghurt is gone. And i lost some weight lol. ### Cpt.Tired — 2015-02-17T20:00:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-155924>

Hello everyone!
I can't stay on the computer for too long since I tend to get migraines from staring at the screen but the "real" migraine inducers are the stimulants - vyvanse and coffee.
Woke up at dawn from a growling stomach, went out on the grass and stared at the blue horizon (the sun wasn't up yet). Cooked some duck sausages and eggs in coconut oil. Took my 50mg of Vyvanse.
Went back to bed.
( The dopamine boost from the vyvanse makes me feel "comfortably tired" is the best way I can describe it.) Got up at 8:30 with an instant coffee (I know its bad, I'm just lazy).
My main problem is FATIGUE!
I will have to come back and add to this journal later on since I am feeling the "headache warning" - some irritation in my pre-frontal cortex which is telling me to drink a bunch of water and relax for a little bit until it passes.

### caroline — 2015-02-18T00:55:00+0200

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-02-18T02:31:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-155939>

I went for a soak in the ocean by my house, about a 30 minute drive then a bit of a hike.
Beautiful day, I was going to take a picture but I left my phone in my car by accident (doh!). It did get rid of my headache. I actually felt pretty decent and relatively energized for the rest of the day! First time I've ever done it. Considering I felt exhausted all day yesterday, this was a nice change.
Those barnacles are nasty though!

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-02-18T19:57:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-155973>

Had fried oysters for breakfast. Lied back down for a while because I feel headachey again.
Then I remember Jack saying in his mitochondrial Rx Q&A webinar, " I woke up feeling like crap so I said 'It's game time'" meaning time for CT.
He felt much better.

So I'm going back to Belcarra Park in Port Moody for a another dip in the ocean. (Jack, are you jealous? LOL! I'm bragging a little.)
Gonna drink a shitload of water before I go. Maybe I'll have some seaweed too for minerals

The time spent is worth it. I got more done yesterday than I had in a while.

People think I'm nuts, but am I really?
No. Just educated.

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-02-18T23:54:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-155985>

I spent a whole 29 minutes up to my chin doing CT in the ocean today!
I actually started to feel strangely comfortable but numb all over. If I stretched my arms out to swim, I would feel a tight, zapping sensation in my for arms and hands.

Getting myself dry afterwards was a challenge since my fingers really didn't work.

I found a nice "private" area off of a trail in Bedwell Bay, just across from Belcarra Park,
That way I didn't have to "explain" to anybody what I was doing.
It really takes your full attention navigating yourself around barnacles and rocks when you are numb, shaking and stiff- at least when you are just starting out like me.

I have to be honest - I don't feel as energized today as I did yesterday. Yesterday I was only able to handle about 4 dips of about 20 - 30 seconds each time but I kept my feet in the water the whole time - about 30 minutes.

Maybe I went a bit overboard today. I don't know.

I feel like I may have a bit of a cold starting. (That would mean my 4th one this year)

Then they send you the results by mail. I was considering it but the nearest lab to me is in Idaho, a bit too far for an easy trip. I'm sure there would be one in Bellingham or nearby though. If you're interested in that route I'll see if I can dig up the info I gathered last year.

I found out my chiropractor has an account with ZRT so I ordered a saliva test panel through him. Speaking of chiropractors, do you see one? The pain relief you describe from the position change makes mw think that it might be something to check into. Mine does decompression therapy which is great for opening up stuck areas. The other thing you might try is ice on the sides of your neck....

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-02-21T04:42:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-156192>

I mentioned it to her and she is aware of EMF and circadian rhythms being important.
She's quite open minded, however I got the feeling that she doesn't know just how important it is.
She said that there is many different angles to treat the same thing and probably the best way is to cover all of it.
I like Jacks angle because its something that is easy for me to understand because of my electricity and physics education.
I think we as humans naturally try to find a single reason or cause for any kind of problem but in reality there are many causes, some more predominate than others at different times.

Anyway, here is some of my history:

I was born in Stockholm, Sweden in 1968, moved with mom and dad to Vancouver, Canada
in 69. (My dad stole my mom from Sweden. He's Canadian,)
I was healthy as a child, no problems there. Grew up in Coquitlam, B.C. a suburb of Vancouver.

Had an eye accident at 11 years old from smashing a pop bottle (dumb). As a result I lost approx 70% vision in my left eye.

Had a bought of OCD/ depression when I was 16 that was undiagnosed because I never sought treatment for it because of its embarrassing nature at the time

That was about June 1984 until sometime around Xmas in seemed to just fade when I got back into school.

Started partying and drinking a fair amount after graduation. The drinking was just on weekends, but it was quite a bit. Had a job doing woodwork.

Started taking a course for avionics at Vancouver International Airport in 1990.
The OCD/depression returned in early '91 during the course, still drinking and partying on the weekends.
(I'm sure the airport was an EMF nightmare! I would have never even thought it was an issue)

Failed the course. Too depressed to do anything about it.
Went back to doing woodwork.

Started taking Fluvoxamine in October '91. After 10 days my depression was gone!
I was so happy but now I was left with chronic fatigue that was a concern but better than the depression which was unbelievably deep.

I stayed on Luvox (and fatigue) for years. I didn't want to chance coming of off it since without it was an absolute nightmare. Still partying and drinking this whole time.

As time went by I started feeling a bit more 'dull' than I used to. I weight trained a lot in the '90s because I was obsessed with women, not having much interest in anything else. I knew there was a bit of a problem there.

In the 2000's I started experimenting with different antidepressants and supplements to try and rid myself of this fatigue.
I was just so sick and tired of having to PUSH myself through a workday when it seemed that everyone else would just breeze through it and still have energy to have a life!

None of the meds worked for the fatigue. My doctor kept on prescribing SSRI's which were all fatiguing, especially PAXIL!!
I started doing my own research and was eyeing up Adderall. I didn't think I had ADHD but I saw that many people said it was great for depression and energy which was exactly what I needed.
I learned about the symptoms of low dopamine and it absolutely described me PERFECTLY!

I asked my GP (Canadian for "MD") if I could try Adderall.
He said "sure".
I was very surprised.
I had been hesitant to ask him for a long time because it was only occasionally used "off label" for depression.
Anyway, it worked AWESOME! I felt like a kid again!

But after about 5 hours that feeling faded.

The next day it faded after 3hrs,
then 1 1/2hr,
then nothing..
....shit.

My doctor increased the dose.. same thing happened.
I maxed out the Adderall at 60mg.

My GP then sent me to an ADHD specialist in North Vancouver.
He assessed me for ADHD and diagnosed me with ADHD/combined type.
(hyperactivity and inattentive).
I asked, "but I'm not hyperactive"
He said, "No, but your BRAIN is."
Yeah, that's true.
So he started me on Vyvanse but the same thing kept happening. He said that I am a fast metabolizer and that I just need a higher dose.
Man - my top dose was 250mg of vyvanse PLUS 3x20mg of adderall!
The same thing kept happening.
We tried Concerta (an extended version of Ritalin)
...it was garbage.
Tried Modafinil, Intuniv,.. garbage...garbage.
Went back to Vyvanse at 150mg because it "worked" the best but not really.
This whole time I've been taking Cipralex (Lexapro) at 10- 20mg.
I started seeing a naturopath earlier this year after doing tons of research and finally realizing that it is NOT quackery and the truth about health has been hidden from us because of politics, drug companies and money.
It took a while for me to see the light but now I'm just pissed at our shitty health care system! I mean the system is good at what it does, no doubt about it.
But the way that it promotes itself as the only thing that works and purposely belittling all other therapies as quackery has really gotten me enraged!

I had a real situation in 2013
My mom had some kind of pelvic floor condition that was driving her crazy.
The doctor just kept telling her "Don't worry, It'll go away".
Around the same time, my dad started to get quite weak, needing help out of the bathtub on occasion.
My mom, frustrated with her condition, starts drinking and smoking quite excessively to
mitigate the stress. (dumb)
My dad starts becoming demented.It starts to really worry my mom and sister (My sister who is disabled with serious OCD, still lives with my parents at this time)
They send him to the ER. They diagnosed him with parkinson's and vascular dementia.
They put him on Zoloft.
The next day he slits his wrist in the wash room because he thought the staff were going to execute him (He survived). My mom at this point REALLY starts escalating her drinking. My sister can't believe all this is happening, so she slits her throat and ends up in the ER too! (She survives).
Now my mom is at home alone. Now I got to do something.
I must have sent her to the hospital about 8- 10 times when I would drive up after work to check up on her and find her so drunk that I was afraid that she would die if I left her alone.
I said "Mom, if you keep this up you will be dead in under 3 months"
It was 2 weeks.
I found her dead on Valentine's Day.
Face down, completely stiff
....just heartbreaking
At this point, I had a LOT of work to do.
Sorry, maybe that was a bit off track
Anyway, my naturopath found that I have extreme adrenal fatigue and thinks my mental symptoms are due to candida overgrowth. I was a milk and cereal junkie until I learned better recently.
Now I have what I think is a stimulant addiction on top of adrenal fatigue and candidiasis.
So I am here trying to dig up some healthy answers for myself because I HATE the side effects of this crap! (The migraines!)
But I keep on taking it because it makes me research like crazy!
Without it, I doubt I would have ever found this site and maintained focus long enough to absorb any information at all!

Gotta go eat. (My wife thinks I'm ignoring her.)

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-02-22T20:37:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-156301>

I do keep lights low at night - definitely. I've been doing that for a long time but I'm really more focused on it since hearing about how crucial it is from Dr Kruse .
I changed some of the main lights that I am exposed to in the evening to those yellow "bug" lights and amber in the computer room.
I also have the f.lux program.
I have to do something about the T.V. upstairs.
My wife comes home at 10 PM and watches T.V. in the living room. She will have no problem falling asleep in front of it but I find it too "stimulating" at night because she will watch programs that are kind of stimulating, like sitcoms that have that "audience laughter" effect. Subconsciously, those programs tend to make you (or me, at least) want to "join in " on the laughter when I'm trying to wind down.
I have a plan, I just haven't done it yet but just me writing this stuff down will help me to follow through with it. I have to put speakers that are close to where she lies so the TV does not have to be so loud PLUS I have to put up a curtain since the TV "flickers" down the hall and compromises my melatonin.

I wonder if that would be like a form of "pulsed" EMF - flickering light from a TV set.

I don't think her sleeping in front of the TV is good for her either! She has M.S.
Her eyes are still going to register those pulses of light through her eyelids.
She says the TV helps her to sleep.

I should put a timer on the TV as well.
(Thinking out loud here)

### Inger — 2015-02-22T20:43:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-156302>

Oh man.. Mike. You need to get rid of that TV. I have no TV and almost never had one either and I am so happy I do not.
If you could massage your wife with magnesium oil before bedtime at candle lights... I am sure that would help her way better to sleep! Magnesium oil is just awesome! It makes one so relaxed... I am sure your wife likes some foot massage
Maybe for a beginning you could talk your wife into having 1 week without TV, just put it somewhere far.. like into the basement.. and then give her massages every evening instead!
Make your own magnesium oil very cheaply with magnesium chloride pharma grade and water

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-02-22T21:18:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quest-to-master-dopamine-journal-for-cpt-tired.12963/post-156308>

I have some magnesium oil. I tried it one night. I put a whole whack of it on my legs. I thought it would help me sleep. I was awake the entire night!
But the interesting thing is - I did NOT get a migraine!
Usually a lack of sleep (more than what is 'normal' now) will trigger a migraine for me
but this time it didn't! I felt not too bad considering the lack of sleep.
But unfortunately it hasn't worked that well for migraines since then.

### Inger — 2015-02-22T21:39:00+0200

and do not forget to drink water

### endless — 2015-02-22T22:57:40+0200
